title: AuthZ latency outlier — validation window & compensation pathway
preview:
From: Novagrid Ops <ops@novagrid.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: sre-lead@novagrid.com, accounts@novagrid.com
Date: Sun, 02 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Spike in authorization latency affecting checkout — observed 09:00–09:23 PST

Hi Chloe,

We saw a sharp increase in authorization latency this morning that impacted our checkout traffic between 09:00 and 09:23 PST. During that window we observed ~18% 5xx responses and elevated 500/504 rates for auth checks; latency p50 jumped from ~45ms to ~2.2s and p95 to ~5.8s. That caused retries on our side and a measurable drop in conversion for affected customers.

We've attached a small log bundle (novagrid_log_bundle_20251102.zip) containing request IDs, timestamps, and example headers. Can you confirm whether this maps to any service-side outage or throttling on Redwood's side? We need to understand SLA credit eligibility and would like an evidence bundle for a potential claim.

Please escalate — we've looped in our SRE and Billing team.

Thanks,
Marcus Lee
Novagrid Ops

Attachment: novagrid_log_bundle_20251102.zip (log bundle, 7.6MB, stub)


From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@novagrid.com, sre-lead@novagrid.com, accounts@novagrid.com
Date: Sun, 02 Nov 2025 09:37:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Spike in authorization latency affecting checkout — observed 09:00–09:23 PST

Ben / David / Laura — forwarding Novagrid's report. Key asks: (1) validate whether there was backend-induced authz latency / throttling for Novagrid's tenant during 2025-11-02 16:00–16:23 UTC, (2) produce a timeline and impact window, and (3) confirm SLA credit eligibility and recommended wording to send back to Novagrid.

Novagrid attached a log bundle; I've also exported the request IDs they included and attached them here (authz-token-latency-traces-11-02.csv). Please prioritize — Novagrid is on a reserved capacity contract (deal RD-ACCT-9843) and has expressed revenue impact.

Proposed recipients for the postmortem invite: Novagrid SRE + Novagrid Billing, Ben, David, Laura, me. Can we get preliminary confirmation by EOD PST today and a final evidence package by EOD Tuesday?

Thanks,
Chloe
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: authz-token-latency-traces-11-02.csv (CSV stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@novagrid.com
Date: Sun, 02 Nov 2025 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Spike in authorization latency affecting checkout — observed 09:00–09:23 PST

Chloe — thanks for the heads up. I've started a quick check against our request logs for Novagrid's tenant during the window. Initial signs show a cluster-level queueing event in the authz frontend shard that correlates with the timestamps they shared. Not yet confirmed whether this was caused by autoscaler churn or a transient kernel scheduling issue.

David — can you pull the tracer spans for the request IDs in the CSV and check for KV cache misses or cross-shard shuffling? If we confirm a service-side latency contribution, we'll mark this as an availability impact for Novagrid and prepare an evidence bundle (logs + traces + aggregated latency histogram).

Ben
Senior SRE, Runtime Reliability

(On an internal note: prioritize spanset INF-7129 and tag as S2 unless we find it was client-side retry behavior.)

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@novagrid.com
Date: Mon, 03 Nov 2025 11:14:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Spike in authorization latency affecting checkout — observed 09:00–09:23 PST

Ben / Chloe — pulled spans and trace aggregates for the listed request IDs. Summary:
- Impact window (UTC): 2025-11-02T16:02:30Z -> 2025-11-02T16:21:45Z (matches Novagrid).
- Root cause indicators: sudden rise in authz RPC wall time due to transient backend thread saturation on authz-frontend-12 (host pool rapid restart following kernel update), result: increased tail latency; no evidence of client-induced retry storm.
- Error types: majority were 502/504 gateway timeouts; small fraction (2-3%) were internal 500s from the authz service.

I've attached a short trace digest and histogram summary in the evidence folder (link in Jira). Classification: availability impact that meets our SLO hit definition for tenant-affecting latency. Recommend we offer proportional remedy under the Dedicated SLA — I estimate credit eligibility pending legal billing review.

I'll prepare the full evidence bundle (aggregated histograms, raw spans, host restart logs) by EOD Tuesday and drop into the Drive link (https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-novagrid-evidence).

-David
Principal Engineer, Observability

Attachment: trace-digest-11-02.txt (trace digest stub)

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lee <ops@novagrid.com>, sre-lead@novagrid.com, accounts@novagrid.com
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 04 Nov 2025 14:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in authorization latency affecting checkout — observed 09:00–09:23 PST

Marcus — thanks for reporting and for the log bundle. Internal investigation confirms a tenant-impacting latency event during 2025-11-02T16:02:30Z–2025-11-02T16:21:45Z caused by an unintended host restart sequence in the authz frontend pool. David and Ben have prepared an evidence package and will deliver the full raw traces + aggregated histograms to the Drive folder by EOD Tuesday.

Next steps and timeline:
- Evidence package: David will upload full traces + host logs to the Drive link by 2025-11-04 23:59 PST (we've created INF-7129 to track).
- Postmortem: proposing Thursday 2025-11-06 11:00 PST / 14:00 EST — invite will include Novagrid SRE, Novagrid Billing, Ben, David, Laura, and me. Please confirm availability so we can finalize the invite.
- Compensation: based on the Dedicated SLA and the confirmed impact window, Novagrid is likely eligible for a proportional credit. Our billing team will calculate the precise credit and follow up by the billing contact copied here.

I appreciate the patience — we'll get the evidence to you and set a clear remediation and prevention plan in the postmortem.

Regards,
Chloe Martin
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments uploaded to Drive: novagrid_evidence/ (raw-spans.tar.gz stub, aggregated-histograms.json stub)

title: Contingency verification: RTO/RPO artifacts & archival manifest
preview:
From: Jennifer Morales <jennifer.morales@aurorafin.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aurora Financial Corp - Compliance Team <compliance@aurorafin.com>
Date: Fri, 31 Oct 2025 09:12:00 +0000
Subject: Request: BCP contingency verification & RTO/RPO artifacts

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick onboarding call yesterday. As part of our SIG/CAIQ follow-up we need an addendum specifically documenting Redwood's BCP/DR posture and evidence for the RTO/RPO commitments you cite in the SIG. Our compliance team would like the following artifacts to close the control: 

- Time-stamped RTO snapshot showing failover/restore duration for the multi-region cache (CSV preferred).
- KV cache warm-up logs for the most recent quarterly drill (NDJSON or logs with timestamps).
- A signed attestation from your Ops/Tech leadership confirming declared RTO/RPO and any caveats (short PDF).
- Archive manifest showing what artifacts (runbooks, backups, key-holders) are retained and where (Excel/CSV).

Target: We need these by EOD PT on Nov 6 to include in our vendor report. Also please identify a technical witness for a 30-minute verification call (Nov 9–11 window).

Can you confirm scope and timeline? If any of these are sensitive, we can set an NDA/secure transfer.

Thanks,
Jen
Jennifer Morales
Senior Manager, Vendor Risk & Compliance
Aurora Financial Corp
compliance@aurorafin.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jennifer Morales <jennifer.morales@aurorafin.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 31 Oct 2025 12:30:45 +0000
Subject: Re: Request: BCP contingency verification & RTO/RPO artifacts

Jen — thanks, and really appreciate the clear checklist. High level: we can provide everything you listed. Quick notes/plan below and attachments sent for intake.

Plan / deliverables:
- rto_snapshot_2025-10-15.csv: time-series extract from our failover test on 2025-10-15 (includes start/finish timestamps and node metrics). Attached.
- kv-cache-warmlog-20251015.ndjson: compacted warming traces for the test, includes token counts and cache-hit progression. Attached as logs.
- signed-bcp-attestation-ben_cto-draft.pdf: internal draft attestation (unsigned) — Neha will finalize and get the CTO's signature. Attached draft for your review.
- contingency-archive-manifest-2025Q3.xlsx: manifest of backup stores, runbook references, and custody owners. Attached.

Witness: Priyom Das (Ops — CC'd) can act as technical witness for the verification call. He can demo the runbook and walk through the metrics. Proposed windows: Nov 9 11:00–11:30 PT or Nov 10 14:00–14:30 PT.

If you prefer secure transfer, we can move the artifacts to a short-lived Drive link with viewer-only access (I included a Drive link below). Let me know which slot works.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0199

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 01 Nov 2025 08:15:00 +0000
Subject: Internal: Aurora request — RTO/RPO evidence (timeline + owner)

Team — Aurora asked for a short deliverable set to close their SIG control. Items attached in this thread; I included the draft attestation but it needs Ops sign-off + a CTO signature. Action items: 

- Priyom: can you validate the rto_snapshot CSV and confirm the failover start/finish rows are correct? Also prepare a 10-min demo script for the witness call.
- Neha: please review the draft attestation, add any caveats (e.g., replicated regions, maintenance windows) and coordinate a signature from the CTO.
- Marissa: confirm the archive manifest ownership column and retention windows; add any references to encrypted S3 buckets and KMS keys.

Target for internal sign-off: Nov 4 COB PT so we can send final PDFs to Aurora on Nov 5 and schedule the witness call. If anything is sensitive, mark files as restricted in the Drive link.

Thanks — Ben

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 04 Nov 2025 16:02:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Internal: Aurora request — RTO/RPO evidence (timeline + owner)

Ben / Priyom — quick status: attached are the finalized artifacts after ops review. Key points below for Aurora's checklist and the attestation language we've agreed on.

Attachments included in this message:
- signed-bcp-attestation-ben_cto-final.pdf (signed by CTO)
- rto_snapshot_2025-10-15.csv (validated; includes node-level recovery times)
- kv-cache-warmlog-20251015.ndjson (sanitized; PII removed)
- contingency-archive-manifest-2025Q3.xlsx (updated owners + S3 path)

Attestation summary (excerpt):
"Redwood Inference maintains a documented BCP and conducts quarterly DR rehearsals. For the multi-region inference stack, the observed median restore time for the Oct 15, 2025 exercise was 82 seconds (system-level) with an RPO of 5 minutes for ephemeral cache state and 24 hours for persisted checkpoints. These figures reflect warm-cache recovery and partial KV replay; full state restoration for cold-start recovery is longer and is documented in the runbook."

We redacted user identifiers from logs and replaced them with deterministic tokens. Priyom — you can own the 30-min witness session. Proposed slots shared privately with Aurora's calendar.

Neha Kapoor
Head of Site Reliability
Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.ai
+1 650-555-0142

title: Audit log provenance, signed manifests, and delivery/escrow options
preview:
From: Jordan Reyes <jordan.reyes@acmehealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: compliance@acmehealth.com
Date: Mon, 3 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: admin activity provenance and export format (SOC2 evidence)

Hi Chloe,

We're preparing our SOC 2 Type II evidence package and need clarity on the admin activity proofs Redwood can provide. Specifically:
- Where do you record administrative actions (console, API, control plane)?
- Can we get an on-demand export for a specified window (UTC start/end) with a signed manifest (SHA256) and per-record provenance (actor, session_id, request_id, backend-node id)?
- Preferred serialization formats (NDJSON, Parquet), gzip/zip, and recommended timezone normalization.
- Any options for offsite escrow or a time-limited SFTP push (encrypted, with HMAC) for handoff to auditors?

We have a compliance deadline next month; can you outline deliverables and expected turnaround for an initial sample extract?

Thanks,
Jordan Reyes
Director, Compliance — AcmeHealth

[Attachment: acme_evidence_request_v1.pdf]

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Reyes <jordan.reyes@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Nov 2025 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: admin activity provenance and export format (SOC2 evidence)

Jordan — thanks for the clear list. High-level answers up front, followed by action items and a proposed sample delivery timeline.

Summary:
- Admin actions: we log admin console actions, API admin calls, and operator actions from the runtime. Logs include actor (email/id), request_id, session_id (when available), operation_type, target_resource, timestamp, and execution node id.
- Provenance: we can produce a per-record provenance line that includes a canonical event_id, request_id, actor, timestamp(UTC), source (console/api/operator), and a signed manifest (SHA256) for the exported file.
- Formats: standard deliverables are NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) gzipped, plus an optional Parquet bundle for large windows. We also provide a CSV summary and a JSON manifest.
- Transport: secure SFTP push with mutual TLS or an encrypted S3 presigned URL are our standard handoff options. We can do scheduled pushes or a one-off on-demand export. Offsite escrow is possible via our partner escrow provider (requires MSA addendum).

Proposed immediate deliverables (sample):
1) 2-day turnaround for a 48-hour sample window (UTC 2025-10-01T00:00:00Z => 2025-10-02T00:00:00Z).
2) Files: admin_events_20251001_48h.ndjson.gz, admin_events_20251001_48h.parquet.gz (if requested), manifest_admin_events_20251001_48h.json, manifest_signed_sha256.txt
3) Manifest includes: file_name, file_size_bytes, sha256_hex, event_count, time_range_utc, timezone_note, production_cluster_id, export_agent_version.
4) Provenance fields per event (flattened): event_id, timestamp_utc, actor_email, actor_internal(bool), request_id, session_id, source, operation, target, node_id, result_code.

Action items:
- Jordan: confirm sample window + preferred transport (SFTP vs presigned S3).
- Kimberly: legal review required for escrow addendum (if Acme picks escrow).
- Ben: prepare the sample extract and include a sample signed manifest.

I'll queue this as high-priority once Jordan confirms.

Best,

To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>

Date: Mon, 3 Nov 2025 11:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: admin activity provenance and export format (SOC2 evidence)

Chloe — quick legal/compliance note for the thread:
- If Acme requests an escrowed delivery (third-party storage of evidence), legal needs the partner's SOC/ISO attestation and a standard addendum.
- For manifests: ensure signatures are generated with our HSM-backed key and include a signing-date header. We will not embed private keys in the export.
- On PII redaction: the export must include a redaction flag per record (redaction_reason) when fields are removed; provide a high-level sample mapping of which fields are redacted by policy.

Jordan — at a minimum we will provide an unredacted sample for Acme's auditors under an NDA; for customer-facing samples we can demonstrate the redacted view. Please note that replays of raw admin events require an approved access request and custodial controls.

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Reyes <jordan.reyes@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Nov 2025 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: admin activity provenance and export format (SOC2 evidence)

Jordan — putting in technical specifics and an export checklist so you know exactly what will appear in the sample bundle.

Export checklist (sample 48-hour window):
- Files produced:
  * admin_events_20251001_48h.ndjson.gz  (gzip, NDJSON; each line = JSON event)
  * admin_events_20251001_48h.parquet.gz (optional; columnar)
  * manifest_admin_events_20251001_48h.json  (human readable manifest)
  * manifest_signed_sha256.txt  (ASCII signature: KEY_ID:sha256_hex; signed_at:2025-11-03T16:44:00Z)
- Event schema (fields included): event_id, timestamp_utc, actor_email, actor_internal, request_id, session_id, source, operation, target_resource, parameters_summary, node_id, result_code, redaction_reason (nullable)
- Provenance assurance:
  * All export bundles are checksummed (sha256) and the manifest is signed with our HSM key.
  * Timestamp normalization: all times converted to UTC and include original tz offset in manifest.
  * We include production_cluster_id and export_agent_version for attestability.
- Transport options supported:
  * SFTP (mutual TLS + AES256) to customer endpoint; supports folder rotation and retention TTL on acceptance.
  * Encrypted S3 presigned PUT URLs (KMS-encrypted) to customer bucket; we support SSE-KMS.
  * Partner escrow: requires signed MSA addendum and a POA to the escrow provider.
- Access controls / audit: every export request is tracked as an admin_export_request event with requester, approver, and TTL. We log who downloaded (if pushed to SFTP we emit a transfer receipt event).

If you confirm SFTP, please provide host/key fingerprint and preferred path. If you want the Parquet bundle, let me know and I will include it; otherwise the NDJSON.gz + signed manifest is standard. I will attach a short sample manifest and a sample sha256 file to the deliverable.

Ben Carter
Principal Engineering — Observability
Redwood Inference

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>

Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Nov 2025 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: admin activity provenance and export format (SOC2 evidence)

Jordan — thanks for the clear list. High-level answers up front, followed by action items and a proposed sample delivery timeline.

Summary:
- Admin actions: we log admin console actions, API admin calls, and operator actions from the runtime. Logs include actor (email/id), request_id, session_id (when available), operation_type, target_resource, timestamp, and execution node id.
- Provenance: we can produce a per-record provenance line that includes a canonical event_id, request_id, actor, timestamp(UTC), source (console/api/operator), and a signed manifest (SHA256) for the exported file.
- Formats: standard deliverables are NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) gzipped, plus an optional Parquet bundle for large windows. We also provide a CSV summary and a JSON manifest.
- Transport: secure SFTP push with mutual TLS or an encrypted S3 presigned URL are our standard handoff options. We can do scheduled pushes or a one-off on-demand export. Offsite escrow is possible via our partner escrow provider (requires MSA addendum).

Proposed immediate deliverables (sample):
1) 2-day turnaround for a 48-hour sample window (UTC 2025-10-01T00:00:00Z => 2025-10-02T00:00:00Z).
2) Files: admin_events_20251001_48h.ndjson.gz, admin_events_20251001_48h.parquet.gz (if requested), manifest_admin_events_20251001_48h.json, manifest_signed_sha256.txt
3) Manifest includes: file_name, file_size_bytes, sha256_hex, event_count, time_range_utc, timezone_note, production_cluster_id, export_agent_version.
4) Provenance fields per event (flattened): event_id, timestamp_utc, actor_email, actor_internal(bool), request_id, session_id, source, operation, target, node_id, result_code.

Action items:
- Jordan: confirm sample window + preferred transport (SFTP vs presigned S3).
- Kimberly: legal review required for escrow addendum (if Acme picks escrow).
- Ben: prepare the sample extract and include a sample signed manifest.

I'll queue this as high-priority once Jordan confirms.

Best,

Redwood Inference

title: Fwd: Support escalation: need model/version change history (interim export until Console UI ships)
preview:
From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Miguel Santos <miguel.santos@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Support escalation: need model/version change history (interim export until Console UI ships)

Hey Chloe,

Escalating a recurring support gap that’s starting to create churn risk with enterprise/Dedicated customers.

Summary:
- We have a live ticket where a customer is reporting output drift and is asking for a definitive answer to: “What model/version did we run yesterday vs today, and what changed?”
- Today, Support can’t reliably self-serve the model change history without asking Eng to manually look across registry/config/releases.
- Even if the change was “non-breaking,” the absence of a customer-facing change log is being interpreted as a silent change.

Most recent example:
- SUP-28429 (Lattice Health Systems) — Dedicated deployment. Reported output regression starting ~Oct 9 18:00 PT. They are pinned to a model alias in their integration, not a hard version.
- SUP-28411 (different enterprise) — asked why a model “seemed faster but different” after a promotion; we couldn’t provide release notes quickly.

What we need (interim, internal-only):
1) A way for Support/TAMs to pull “model/version change history” for a given account + deployment mode (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) + time range.
2) Ideally exportable to CSV so we can attach to the ticket + share a sanitized version with customer.
3) Must not leak internal-only notes (esp. the root-cause / rollout notes that are fine for admins but not customers).

We know the proper Console UI is coming (ENG-9824), but we need a stopgap for the next few weeks. Even an internal endpoint or script + runbook would help.

Can you advise who owns the fastest path for an internal export? Happy to help test with real cases.

Thanks,
Dev

—
Dev Patel
Support Escalations
Redwood Inference

Attachment: SUP-28429-summary.txt



From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Miguel Santos <miguel.santos@redwoodinference.com>, Jada Williams <jada.williams@redwoodinference.com>, Michael Nguyen <michael.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: Support escalation: need model/version change history (interim export until Console UI ships)

Dev — thanks for sending with concrete tickets.

Agree this is a gap (and aligns with what we’re building in Console), but we should be able to give you an interim internal tool/export.

A couple quick clarifying questions so we don’t ship the wrong thing:
- For Dedicated, do you need this by “account id” (customer org) and “deployment id/cluster”, or is org-level enough?
- When you say “model alias,” are they pinned to something like redwood://models/<family>:latest, or are they using our routing policy layer (i.e., eligible promotion changes under the hood)?
- Do you need per-request history (what model served each request), or the list of promotion events (what changed and when)?

Looping in Jada + Michael because they’re closest to the eventing/changelog work. My intuition: the fastest path is to query the model promotion/audit events we’re emitting and expose a locked-down admin endpoint (Support RBAC) + CSV export.

We should also be careful here given the recent issue where internal notes briefly showed up in rollout UI (PR-18463). Any interim solution needs redaction baked in.

Dev/Miguel — can you reply with:
1) the customer org slug / internal account id for Lattice
2) the exact time window where they saw drift
3) what you want the customer-facing narrative to include (just “version changed,” or high-level perf/quality category)

Chloe

—
Chloe Martin
Engineering Manager, Console Backend
Redwood Inference



From: Miguel Santos <miguel.santos@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jada Williams <jada.williams@redwoodinference.com>, Michael Nguyen <michael.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 10:22 AM
Subject: Re: Support escalation: need model/version change history (interim export until Console UI ships)

Adding CS context (I’m the CS lead on Lattice):

- They’re Dedicated, single region (us-west-2), cluster “lat-prod-01” (from our onboarding doc).
- They use the alias “rw.chat/atlas-13b” (their code references the alias; they did not pin a specific semantic version).
- They’re asking for:
  a) “Did you change the model?” (yes/no)
  b) “When did you change it?” (timestamp)
  c) “What changed / how risky?” (high-level, not internal details)
  d) “How do we roll back?” (instructions or we do it for them)

Time window:
- First report: Oct 9 ~6:10pm PT
- Most acute: Oct 9 6:30pm–8:00pm PT

What we need quickly is the promotion/change event view. Per-request history would be nice but not required for this ticket.

If we can get a CSV export with: timestamp, alias, old_version, new_version, deployment_id/cluster, and a public-safe summary category, we can respond confidently.

Miguel

—
Miguel Santos
Enterprise Customer Success
Redwood Inference



From: Jada Williams <jada.williams@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Miguel Santos <miguel.santos@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Nguyen <michael.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 11:08 AM
Subject: Re: Support escalation: need model/version change history (interim export until Console UI ships)

+1 that “promotion events only” gets us 80% of the value quickly.

What we have today:
- Control plane is emitting structured events for model metadata changes + version promotions (this is the pipeline we’re using for the changelog generator work).
- Internally, we can query the event store, but there isn’t a Support-friendly surface.

Fastest interim option (1–2 days):
- Michael and I can provide a one-off script + runbook that:
  - takes org/account id + deployment id + time range
  - queries promotion events (alias -> concrete version) and deprecation/rollback events
  - applies the public/internal redaction rules (i.e., only include fields flagged public)
  - outputs CSV

Near-term productized option (1–2 weeks, but behind admin RBAC):
- Add an internal-only endpoint in Console backend (or control plane admin) like:
  GET /admin/model-change-events?account_id=...&deployment_id=...&since=...&until=...
- Response includes a “public_summary” field that can be safely copied into a customer email (aligned to release note taxonomy categories).

Constraints:
- For Dedicated, we need to be careful about “deployment_id” vs “cluster name” mapping, but we can start with deployment_id and add lookup.
- We should explicitly exclude any internal_only notes and anything that mentions capacity/incident context.

Dev/Miguel: if you send me the internal account_id (or org slug) for Lattice, I can have Michael run the event query for the Oct 9 window today.

Jada

—
Jada Williams
Staff Engineer, Console & Rollouts
Redwood Inference



From: Michael Nguyen <michael.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jada Williams <jada.williams@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Miguel Santos <miguel.santos@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 1:34 PM
Subject: Re: Support escalation: need model/version change history (interim export until Console UI ships)

text:
From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Miguel Santos <miguel.santos@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Support escalation: need model/version change history (interim export until Console UI ships)

Hey Chloe,

Escalating a recurring support gap that’s starting to create churn risk with enterprise/Dedicated customers.

Summary:
- We have a live ticket where a customer is reporting output drift and is asking for a definitive answer to: “What model/version did we run yesterday vs today, and what changed?”
- Today, Support can’t reliably self-serve the model change history without asking Eng to manually look across registry/config/releases.
- Even if the change was “non-breaking,” the absence of a customer-facing change log is being interpreted as a silent change.

Most recent example:
- SUP-28429 (Lattice Health Systems) — Dedicated deployment. Reported output regression starting ~Oct 9 18:00 PT. They are pinned to a model alias in their integration, not a hard version.
- SUP-28411 (different enterprise) — asked why a model “seemed faster but different” after a promotion; we couldn’t provide release notes quickly.

What we need (interim, internal-only):
1) A way for Support/TAMs to pull “model/version change history” for a given account + deployment mode (Hosted/Dedicated/Private) + time range.
2) Ideally exportable to CSV so we can attach to the ticket + share a sanitized version with customer.
3) Must not leak internal-only notes (esp. the root-cause / rollout notes that are fine for admins but not customers).

We know the proper Console UI is coming (ENG-9824), but we need a stopgap for the next few weeks. Even an internal endpoint or script + runbook would help.

Can you advise who owns the fastest path for an internal export? Happy to help test with real cases.

Thanks,
Dev

—
Dev Patel
Support Escalations
Redwood Inference

Attachment: SUP-28429-summary.txt



From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Miguel Santos <miguel.santos@redwoodinference.com>, Jada Williams <jada.williams@redwoodinference.com>, Michael Nguyen <michael.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: Support escalation: need model/version change history (interim export until Console UI ships)

Dev — thanks for sending with concrete tickets.

Agree this is a gap (and aligns with what we’re building in Console), but we should be able to give you an interim internal tool/export.

A couple quick clarifying questions so we don’t ship the wrong thing:
- For Dedicated, do you need this by “account id” (customer org) and “deployment id/cluster”, or is org-level enough?
- When you say “model alias,” are they pinned to something like redwood://models/<family>:latest, or are they using our routing policy layer (i.e., eligible promotion changes under the hood)?
- Do you need per-request history (what model served each request), or the list of promotion events (what changed and when)?

Looping in Jada + Michael because they’re closest to the eventing/changelog work. My intuition: the fastest path is to query the model promotion/audit events we’re emitting and expose a locked-down admin endpoint (Support RBAC) + CSV export.

We should also be careful here given the recent issue where internal notes briefly showed up in rollout UI (PR-18463). Any interim solution needs redaction baked in.

Dev/Miguel — can you reply with:
1) the customer org slug / internal account id for Lattice
2) the exact time window where they saw drift
3) what you want the customer-facing narrative to include (just “version changed,” or high-level perf/quality category)

Chloe

—
Chloe Martin
Engineering Manager, Console Backend
Redwood Inference



From: Miguel Santos <miguel.santos@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jada Williams <jada.williams@redwoodinference.com>, Michael Nguyen <michael.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 10:22 AM
Subject: Re: Support escalation: need model/version change history (interim export until Console UI ships)

Adding CS context (I’m the CS lead on Lattice):

- They’re Dedicated, single region (us-west-2), cluster “lat-prod-01” (from our onboarding doc).
- They use the alias “rw.chat/atlas-13b” (their code references the alias; they did not pin a specific semantic version).
- They’re asking for:
  a) “Did you change the model?” (yes/no)
  b) “When did you change it?” (timestamp)
  c) “What changed / how risky?” (high-level, not internal details)
  d) “How do we roll back?” (instructions or we do it for them)

Time window:
- First report: Oct 9 ~6:10pm PT
- Most acute: Oct 9 6:30pm–8:00pm PT

What we need quickly is the promotion/change event view. Per-request history would be nice but not required for this ticket.

If we can get a CSV export with: timestamp, alias, old_version, new_version, deployment_id/cluster, and a public-safe summary category, we can respond confidently.

Miguel

—
Miguel Santos
Enterprise Customer Success
Redwood Inference



From: Jada Williams <jada.williams@redwoodinference.com>

Cc: Michael Nguyen <michael.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 11:08 AM
Subject: Re: Support escalation: need model/version change history (interim export until Console UI ships)

+1 that “promotion events only” gets us 80% of the value quickly.

What we have today:
- Control plane is emitting structured events for model metadata changes + version promotions (this is the pipeline we’re using for the changelog generator work).
- Internally, we can query the event store, but there isn’t a Support-friendly surface.

Fastest interim option (1–2 days):
- Michael and I can provide a one-off script + runbook that:
  - takes org/account id + deployment id + time range
  - queries promotion events (alias -> concrete version) and deprecation/rollback events
  - applies the public/internal redaction rules (i.e., only include fields flagged public)
  - outputs CSV

Near-term productized option (1–2 weeks, but behind admin RBAC):
- Add an internal-only endpoint in Console backend (or control plane admin) like:
  GET /admin/model-change-events?account_id=...&deployment_id=...&since=...&until=...
- Response includes a “public_summary” field that can be safely copied into a customer email (aligned to release note taxonomy categories).

Constraints:
- For Dedicated, we need to be careful about “deployment_id” vs “cluster name” mapping, but we can start with deployment_id and add lookup.
- We should explicitly exclude any internal_only notes and anything that mentions capacity/incident context.

Dev/Miguel: if you send me the internal account_id (or org slug) for Lattice, I can have Michael run the event query for the Oct 9 window today.

Jada

Jada Williams
Staff Engineer, Console & Rollouts
Redwood Inference

- Demo quota: you mentioned a weekly cap of 8 demo seats for initial launch. We need to confirm whether those are per-region or global.

title: Post-demo: model fit prioritization & parity checks (SageBank)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@sagebank.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 27 Sep 2025 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: model fit prioritization & parity checks (SageBank)

Thanks again for taking the demo today — good conversation. This thread captures the items we agreed to follow up on and my recommended next steps for a short eval. Please correct me if I missed anything.

Recap / goals (from our call)
- Primary goal: replace SageBank's current proprietary ranking pipeline for customer support routing with an open-model-based candidate that meets latency and accuracy parity while lowering inference cost.
- Acceptance signals: macro accuracy on the labeled routing set, top-1 routing accuracy within 2 percentage points of the proprietary baseline, and no critical regression on the high-risk subset (fraud/AML).

Ben's recommended short eval (2-week sprint)
1) Candidate models to trial: Llama-2-70b-chat (quantized fp16), Mosaic-Base-34b, and a tuned Llama-2-13b as a low-latency baseline.
2) Benchmarks to run: latency p50/p95 at your target qps (attached script + sample prompt bundle), accuracy on the 2.5k labeled routing set, and focused regressions on the 120 high-risk cases you shared.
3) Prompt/shot set: attached prompt-bundle CSV (includes warmup prefixes, reranking prompts, and de-duplication heuristics).
4) Acceptance gates: pass accuracy within 2pp top-1 vs baseline; no more than 1% absolute increase in false positives on high-risk subset; average token/time cost at or below current per-request spend.

Next steps (owners & timing)
- Ben: provide prompt bundle and parity benchmark script (today) — attached.
- Monica: setup a short Dedicated trial instance with 2 gpus and our canonical batching config (Mon, 9/29).
- Alicia/Tom: share the labeled routing set and the 120 high-risk cases by EOD Monday 9/29.
- Marissa: align on commercial guardrails and an initial SOW draft if the trial looks good (wk of 10/6).

Open questions you raised
- How we handle KV cache warm starts for very short contexts (we recommend prefix caching — details in attached notes).
- If parity is close but not exact: recommend a hybrid fallback rule to the proprietary model for top-critical flows during canary.

Attachments: redwood_eval_prompt-bundle-v1.csv, parity-benchmark-results.pdf (sample format)

Thanks — I'll keep this thread as the central log for the sprint.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192



From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@sagebank.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@sagebank.com>
Date: Sun, 28 Sep 2025 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: model fit prioritization & parity checks (SageBank)

Thanks Ben — appreciate the clear plan. A couple of clarifying items from our side before we hand data over: 

1) Acceptance thresholds: the product team insists on a maximum 1pp drop in top-1 routing accuracy for non-critical traffic, and zero tolerance for regressions in the fraud/AML slice (that must match or be better). Can you confirm if the 2pp window you listed is flexible?

2) Cost expectations: we need a conservative target — ideally 20% reduction in per-request inference spend; if the open models only get us to 5-10% savings vs our proprietary setup, trading off accuracy won't fly.

3) Latency: our target p95 is <= 350ms at average QPS of 80. If Llama-2-70b with quantization can hit accuracy but not latency, we'd prefer a smaller model that meets latency.

We can share the labeled set and the 120 high-risk cases but will need an NDA addendum for the AML examples — legal will send a short DocuSign today. Also, Tom asked whether Redwood can provide a pre-run of the parity script against a small shadow set (say 200 samples) so we can see the format and quick signal before committing full data.

Tom and I are aligned on priorities: fraud/AML guarantees first, then overall cost. If you can produce a short pre-run and adjust the acceptance gates to reflect a stricter fraud requirement, we're good to proceed.

—
Alicia Gomez
Head of Customer Automation | SageBank
alicia.gomez@sagebank.com

On Sun, Sep 28, 2025 at 10:02 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks — sending the prompt bundle now.
> 
> Ben



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@sagebank.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@sagebank.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Sep 2025 09:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: model fit prioritization & parity checks (SageBank)

Appreciate the callout, Alicia — good to have the stricter fraud requirement captured. Quick answers and an updated plan below.

1) Acceptance thresholds: we can tighten the gate to match your ask — zero regression tolerated on the AML slice; for non-critical traffic we’ll require <=1pp drop in top-1 accuracy versus baseline. I updated the acceptance gates in the Drive doc (link below).

2) Pre-run: attached parity-benchmark-results.pdf contains a 200-sample pre-run on your anonymized shadow set (we used Mosaic-Base-34b and Llama-2-13b). The full parity script is in the Drive link; the output CSV mirrors the schema we’ll use for the sprint.

3) Latency/cost tradeoffs: recommendation — run two parallel trials: a performance-first lane (Llama-2-13b tuned + batching) and a quality-first lane (Llama-2-70b quantized). We’ll measure p50/p95 and per-request token cost and present a comparison matrix after week 1. If the 70b meets accuracy but misses latency, we can propose a hybrid route: route high-risk flows to the tuned 13b+fallback or keep proprietary for the highest-risk subset while shifting the rest to the open model.

4) Next concrete steps (updated owners/timing)
- Ben: deliver full Drive folder with scripts, sample outputs, and an updated acceptance gate doc (done) — https://drive.redwood.com/sagebank/evals/2025-09
- Monica: trial infra ready by EOD Mon 9/29; will share endpoint details and a small smoke test payload.
- Alicia: legal NDAs — once signed, send the 2.5k labeled set + 120 cases. If you prefer, we can accept a hashed/anonymized version for initial runs.
- Marissa: we’ll prepare an interim SOW that includes a canary fallback and a 30-day rollback window; will share draft by 10/3.

Notes on the pre-run results (attached PDF)
- Mosaic-Base-34b: quick-win on latency (p95 ~ 310ms) and modest accuracy lift vs our tuned 13b on the shadow set, cost ~12% lower than your current stack in these sample runs.
- Llama-2-13b tuned: best latency (p95 ~ 180ms), lower token cost, slightly lower macro accuracy; looked promising as an immediate replacement for low-latency paths.

If this all looks good, I’ll mark the trial kickoff for Tue 9/30 and share the endpoint + scripts for your data ingestion. Want to be mindful of Regina in legal — happy to loop in if you need a quick signature path.

Thanks — will follow up after Monica posts the trial endpoints.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@sagebank.com>

Date: Sun, 28 Sep 2025 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: model fit prioritization & parity checks (SageBank)

Thanks Ben — appreciate the clear plan. A couple of clarifying items from our side before we hand data over: 

1) Acceptance thresholds: the product team insists on a maximum 1pp drop in top-1 routing accuracy for non-critical traffic, and zero tolerance for regressions in the fraud/AML slice (that must match or be better). Can you confirm if the 2pp window you listed is flexible?

2) Cost expectations: we need a conservative target — ideally 20% reduction in per-request inference spend; if the open models only get us to 5-10% savings vs our proprietary setup, trading off accuracy won't fly.

3) Latency: our target p95 is <= 350ms at average QPS of 80. If Llama-2-70b with quantization can hit accuracy but not latency, we'd prefer a smaller model that meets latency.

We can share the labeled set and the 120 high-risk cases but will need an NDA addendum for the AML examples — legal will send a short DocuSign today. Also, Tom asked whether Redwood can provide a pre-run of the parity script against a small shadow set (say 200 samples) so we can see the format and quick signal before committing full data.

Tom and I are aligned on priorities: fraud/AML guarantees first, then overall cost. If you can produce a short pre-run and adjust the acceptance gates to reflect a stricter fraud requirement, we're good to proceed.

—
Alicia Gomez
Head of Customer Automation | SageBank
alicia.gomez@sagebank.com

Subject: Re: Post-demo: model fit prioritization & parity checks (SageBank)

Appreciate the callout, Alicia — good to have the stricter fraud requirement captured. Quick answers and an updated plan below.

1) Acceptance thresholds: we can tighten the gate to match your ask — zero regression tolerated on the AML slice; for non-critical traffic we’ll require <=1pp drop in top-1 accuracy versus baseline. I updated the acceptance gates in the Drive doc (link below).

2) Pre-run: attached parity-benchmark-results.pdf contains a 200-sample pre-run on your anonymized shadow set (we used Mosaic-Base-34b and Llama-2-13b). The full parity script is in the Drive link; the output CSV mirrors the schema we’ll use for the sprint.

3) Latency/cost tradeoffs: recommendation — run two parallel trials: a performance-first lane (Llama-2-13b tuned + batching) and a quality-first lane (Llama-2-70b quantized). We’ll measure p50/p95 and per-request token cost and present a comparison matrix after week 1. If the 70b meets accuracy but misses latency, we can propose a hybrid route: route high-risk flows to the tuned 13b+fallback or keep proprietary for the highest-risk subset while shifting the rest to the open model.

4) Next concrete steps (updated owners/timing)
- Ben: deliver full Drive folder with scripts, sample outputs, and an updated acceptance gate doc (done) — https://drive.redwood.com/sagebank/evals/2025-09
- Monica: trial infra ready by EOD Mon 9/29; will share endpoint details and a small smoke test payload.
- Alicia: legal NDAs — once signed, send the 2.5k labeled set + 120 cases. If you prefer, we can accept a hashed/anonymized version for initial runs.
- Marissa: we’ll prepare an interim SOW that includes a canary fallback and a 30-day rollback window; will share draft by 10/3.

Notes on the pre-run results (attached PDF)
- Mosaic-Base-34b: quick-win on latency (p95 ~ 310ms) and modest accuracy lift vs our tuned 13b on the shadow set, cost ~12% lower than your current stack in these sample runs.
- Llama-2-13b tuned: best latency (p95 ~ 180ms), lower token cost, slightly lower macro accuracy; looked promising as an immediate replacement for low-latency paths.

If this all looks good, I’ll mark the trial kickoff for Tue 9/30 and share the endpoint + scripts for your data ingestion. Want to be mindful of Regina in legal — happy to loop in if you need a quick signature path.

Thanks — will follow up after Monica posts the trial endpoints.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

title: Marketplace list-price holdback & partner crediting for private offer
preview:
From: Jenna Park <jenna.park@acme-retail.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement <procurement@acme-retail.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Sep 2025 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Marketplace pricing: list-price holdback and partner crediting

Hi Aditya / Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the team was impressed with throughput and the cost controls. We are moving faster than expected on the procurement side and want to get a Private Offer ready for AWS and a parallel path for GCP. A couple of asks from procurement before we put the PO in: 

- Confirmation of Redwood's approach to list-price \"holdback\" when provisioning through AWS Marketplace (they want an explicit model showing the list price vs. net-back to Redwood and to partners).
- How channel partner crediting (reseller passback) will be represented on the AWS Private Offer and whether that differs for GCP Marketplace.
- Any marketplace fees or handling fees we should put in the PO so finance can do a quick model.

We have an indicative commit: 500k ARR across AWS + GCP over the first 12 months (split TBD). Procurement has attached a short workbook with expected invoice cadence and some questions (Acme_PO_Details.xlsx). Can you send a sketch of the proposed economics and an example of the private-offer fields we should expect in the AWS template?

Goal: get aligned this week and lock in a signature meeting next Wednesday.

Thanks,
Jenna Park
Director, Cloud Procurement
Acme Retail

Attachments: Acme_PO_Details.xlsx


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jenna Park <jenna.park@acme-retail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Sep 2025 13:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace pricing: list-price holdback and partner crediting

Hi Jenna — really appreciate the fast follow.

High-level answers up front so procurement can model quickly:
- We publish a list price in the marketplace listing. For private offers, the PO will reference a list price but the net-back to Redwood is typically the list price minus marketplace fees and any partner/reseller passback.
- Typical split we see on deals this size is marketplace fee 6–9% (variable by cloud) + partner passback 8–15% depending on the partner tier and negotiated commission. The remaining net is Redwood revenue.
- For GCP the marketplace fee mechanics differ (usually an invoice credit model) — we'll attach an AWS private-offer template and a sample net-back worksheet.

I've attached a quick estimate and a sketch of a channel-split we think will be acceptable to Acme based on the commitment (redwood_po_pricing_v1.pdf). Proposed working model (for illustration only):
- List price: $1,000/unit (example)
- AWS marketplace fee: 7%
- Partner passback: 12%
- Redwood net-back: 81% (post-fee/passback)

If that looks directionally right, we can convert to a per-SKU model and generate an AWS Private Offer draft (we will populate the AWS template fields that procurement needs). Can we hop on a 30-min sync tomorrow morning to get the commit split and partner identity?

Best,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0172

Attachments: redwood_po_pricing_v1.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Sep 2025 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Marketplace pricing: list-price holdback and partner crediting

Forwarding Jenna's PO workbook — procurement wants explicit line items for marketplace holdback and partner credits.

Kimberly — quick thoughts from rev ops: we should avoid presenting the partner passback as a line-item that reduces the list price on the marketplace entry itself. That creates visibility and could trigger unexpected tax or resale implications on Acme's side. Instead:
- Use list price as the canonical marketplace SKU price.
- Represent partner passback as a contractual rebate (settled monthly/quarterly) or as a pass-through line on the private-offer terms that is visible to Acme and AWS but not as a permanent list-price reduction.

Also: watch for tax treatment on passbacks — if the partner is receiving a passback directly from Redwood it may change invoicing. If the partner is paid by Acme (e.g., reseller rebate) the PO mechanics differ.

Ben
VP, Marketplace Sales | Redwood Inference

--- Forwarded message from Jenna Park <jenna.park@acme-retail.com> ---
(Attached: Acme_PO_Details.xlsx)


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Sep 2025 10:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace pricing: list-price holdback and partner crediting

Ben — agreed on the rebate vs list-price visibility approach. A couple of operational notes for Aditya / Jenna: 

- AWS Private Offer fields: we'll include an \"Invoice Adjustment\" field in our internal PO mapping and then a separate contractual appendix that documents the partner passback percentage and payment cadence. That lets Acme see the net economics in one place while keeping marketplace listing simple.
- Recommended starting point for channel splits (given the $500k blended commit): marketplace fee 7% (assume AWS), partner passback 10% (proposal), Redwood net-back 83%. If Acme insists on a partner-visible line we can map it to a periodic rebate.
- For GCP, recommend we model fees as credits because GCP doesn't flow marketplace fees the same way; treat it as an account-level credit on the invoice.

I'll attach the AWS Private Offer template and a drive reference showing how we map line-items for finance.

Kimberly Park
Director, Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: AWS_Private_Offer_Template.docx
Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1a2b3cAcmePORef


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jenna Park <jenna.park@acme-retail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, procurement <procurement@acme-retail.com>, AWS Marketplace <aws-marketplace@amazon.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace pricing: list-price holdback & partner crediting for private offer

Hi Jenna — thanks for sending the workbook. Per our internal ops review (Ben / Kimberly looped in), here's a proposed, actionable structure you can give to finance and AWS: 

1) List price: Redwood will publish the SKU at the list price referenced in the PO (we used the example in our sketch).
2) Marketplace fees: estimate at 7% for AWS; GCP modeled as invoice credit (we'll provide an example).
3) Partner passback: proposed 10% of list price, paid as a quarterly rebate from Redwood to the named partner. We will not reduce the marketplace list price to reflect the partner passback; instead, we will document the passback in the private-offer appendix and in the contract so Acme's finance team sees the net economics.
4) Tax/Invoice handling: if Acme wants partner paid directly by Redwood, that will require Redwood to issue a separate rebate invoice and could change VAT/sales tax treatment. If Acme prefers partner paid by Acme via a separate line in their internal procurement flows, that's fine and simpler for tax treatment.

Attached: redwood_po_pricing_v1.pdf (illustrative net-back table) and AWS_Private_Offer_Template.docx (fields we will fill when we create the private offer).

Next steps:
- Can we lock a 30-min call today (3pm PT) or tomorrow morning to confirm the partner identity and exact commit split between AWS and GCP?
- If procurement wants to proceed to signature, we'll draft the Private Offer and a short appendix describing the passback mechanics and tax options.

Looking forward —
Aditya Rao
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0172

Attachments: redwood_po_pricing_v1.pdf, AWS_Private_Offer_Template.docx

From: Amrita Desai <amrita@finlytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops@finlytics.com
Date: Thu, 18 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Evening soak — quick top-up request

Hi Ben,

Quick note from our ops run this morning: the evening soak (scheduled 09/19 22:00 PT) for our microburst scenario shows we’ll likely exceed the trial allowance by ~36 hours of sustained token generation. We want to keep the run overnight to validate 99th-percentile latency under steady traffic.

Can we get a short supplemental credit top-up for that window (48h scope, ~6M tokens)? If useful I attached the trimmed POC plan and a usage snapshot from the last dry run.

What we need:
- 48h uplift effective 2025-09-19 22:00 PT
- Prefer a safety buffer (10%) for retries

We’re on a tight timeline to finalize SLA numbers for the enterprise pilot — appreciate any fast path you can offer.

Thanks,
Amrita Desai
Head of Platform — Finlytics
amrita@finlytics.com

Attachment: night-snapshot-20250918.csv (text/csv)

preview:

Subject: Request: reduce dedicated capacity mid-billing cycle (proration & VAT)

Hi Ben / Marissa,

We need to make a mid-month change to our Dedicated plan. Effective 2025-10-01 we want to reduce reserved capacity from 8 GPUs to 6 GPUs (we're pausing two machines as part of a workload consolidation). Can you confirm how Redwood handles proration for mid-cycle capacity reductions and whether the credit will show as a separate line on the next invoice?

Also: CloudMetrics is US-registered (EIN 12-3456789). Please confirm whether any VAT / tax adjustments apply for this credit and whether you need a new PO or revised PO number from our end. I'm attaching our original PO and the current invoice for reference.

Requested by our finance team — month-end close is 10/05, so if possible we need the credit to appear on the October invoice.

Attachments: purchase_order_CM-789.pdf (PDF, 45KB), cloudmetrics_invoice_0925.pdf (PDF, 120KB)

Thanks,
Olivia Chen
Head of Cloud Engineering
CloudMetrics, Inc.
olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com
+1 (415) 555-0123


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Sep 2025 11:03:22 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: reduce dedicated capacity mid-billing cycle (proration & VAT)

Hi Olivia — thanks for the details and the PO. Looping in Priya (billing) and Rishi (finance) to confirm exact credit timing and tax handling. Quick notes from my side:

- We can do a mid-cycle proration for the 2 GPUs you are removing. Redwood prorates on a per-GPU-day basis, then applies the credit to your next invoice.
- If you need the credit to post on the October invoice (closing 10/05), please confirm the effective date as 2025-10-01 and provide the PO number you want referenced on the credit memo (I see PO CM-789 attached).
- As a ballpark: current monthly cost for 8 GPUs = $32,000. Removing 2 GPUs (25% reduction) effective 10/01 yields ~25% of a one-month value prorated for the unused days in October. I ran a quick calc and attached a spreadsheet — estimated credit: $2,580 (rounded). See proration_calc_0925.xlsx.

Priya/Rishi: please confirm exact rounding rules, whether we post as a credit memo or negative invoice line, and VAT/tax steps. Olivia: if you want the credit earlier than the next invoice, we can issue a one-off credit memo but that will still appear as a credit on our accounting and should be visible via your billing page link.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Enterprise Accounts
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | (650) 555-0199


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@cloudmetrics.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Sep 2025 08:15:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: reduce dedicated capacity mid-billing cycle (proration & VAT)

Hi Olivia — thanks for the heads up and the PO. A few clarifications and what we'll do next: 

1) Policy / approach:
   - Mid-cycle reductions are prorated on a per-GPU-day basis and result in a credit on the next posted invoice (we do not reissue the original invoice).
   - Credits appear as a separate credit memo line ("Prorated capacity adjustment") and will show on the next invoice due to you.

2) Timing:
   - You confirmed effective date 2025-10-01: that works. The proration window is 2025-10-01 through 2025-10-31 (30 days) but we compute unused GPU-days from that effective date relative to your billing period (2025-10-01 to 2025-10-31). The credit will post on the invoice generated 2025-10-25 (your monthly invoice for Oct). It will be reflected in the payment due on 2025-11-05.

3) Amounts / rounding:
   - We calculate at the line-item level. Example (based on posted rate): 8 GPUs @ $4,000/GPU-month = $32,000. Removing 2 GPUs = $8,000/month; for 31 days in Oct the unused portion is 31 days * (2 GPUs * daily rate). Attached is the detailed calc (proration_calc_0925.xlsx). Final credit will be rounded to nearest dollar.

4) Tax/VAT handling:
   - Since CloudMetrics is US-registered (EIN provided), we will not apply EU VAT. Redwood will apply US sales tax only where required by jurisdiction; per our records CloudMetrics billing address is CA, so sales tax may apply. If you prefer a zero-rated tax treatment, please send a written resale/exemption certificate.

5) Required info from CloudMetrics to finalize:
   - Confirm effective date (REPLY: yes/no)
   - Confirm PO number to reference on the credit memo (we will use CM-789 if that is OK)
   - If you need the credit earlier than the standard next invoice, confirm and we can request expedited processing (subject to internal approvals).

I attached a draft "adjusted invoice" stub showing the credit line that will be inserted (cloudmetrics_invoice_0925.pdf).

Regards,
Priya Natarajan
Billing & Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

We do need the credit to appear on the October invoice (your 10/25 run) because our month-end close is 10/05 and accounting needs visibility in the October ledger.

A couple small asks:
- Can you provide the proration broken out per line item (GPU1, GPU2) as your finance team prefers line-level details for posting?
- Please confirm whether the credit memo will include the PO number and the CLD deal id (CLD-2025-0891) so we can match it to our AP system.

If you can expedite the credit memo issuance that would be helpful. I attached the PO again for quick reference.

Attachments: purchase_order_CM-789.pdf

Appreciate the quick turnaround — let me know if you need anything else.

Olivia

Subject: MAP: unit-econ snapshot & eval plan - pilots to close timeline

Hi Sara,

Thanks again for taking the demo last week. As we talked about, the mutual action plan (MAP) for the pilot needs a short cost/econ checkpoint so your procurement and engineering teams can see the path to a production unit price.

To keep the MAP moving toward a Oct 15 close target, can you confirm the three inputs below for the pilot workload? We'll plug them into the attached cost model and return a tokens-per-dollar sensitivity table.

1) Daily request volume (avg and 95th pct) and expected avg prompt + response tokens per request.
2) Expected percent of calls that will hit cached prefixes / persistent KV cache (estimate).
3) Latency SLO target that matters for you (e.g., sub-300ms p50 or sub-800ms p95) — this determines batching tradeoffs.

I attached an initial spreadsheet (pulse_cost_model_v1.xlsx) and a short eval plan draft (pulse_eval_plan_v1.md) — these include recommended batching and cache assumptions we typically use for clinical-document workflows. If you can share the numbers above by Wednesday EOD, we'll turn around a revised MAP cost page and a rollout gating checklist for legal/procurement.

Thanks — quick next steps: Sara to confirm numbers, Redwood to return tokens/$ table + MAP milestone dates.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: pulse_cost_model_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), pulse_eval_plan_v1.md (plain text)


From: Sara Jenkins <sara.jenkins@pulsehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Sep 2025 11:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: unit-econ snapshot & eval plan - pilots to close timeline

Ben,

Appreciate the clear ask. Quick answers below and I've attached our senior PM's volume spreadsheet for visibility.

- Daily avg requests (pilot): 18,000 (95th pct burst to ~45k)
- Avg prompt tokens: 420; avg response tokens: 310 (so ~730 tokens total per call)
- Estimated cache hit (prefix + KV reuse): 22% initially, expect 35% after warm-up + templating
- Latency SLO: p95 < 700ms is what product team needs to validate the UX. p50 < 250ms ideal.

We want to keep the pilot cost target to under $0.007 / effective user request (after cache/batching) to make the unit econ work in our model. Procurement also wants a simple per-100k-tokens and per-embedding line item for the MAP.

Sending over the PM sheet now (pm_pulse_volume_sep.xlsx) and cc'ing our procurement lead if needed.

Thanks,
Sara Jenkins
Sr Product Manager | Pulse Health
sara.jenkins@pulsehealth.com

Attachment referenced: pm_pulse_volume_sep.xlsx


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Sep 2025 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: MAP: unit-econ snapshot & eval plan - pilots to close timeline

Karthik / Monica — forwarding Sara's numbers. Important callouts I want us to align on before we return the MAP page:

- Baseline tokens/call ~730; 18k avg/day with 95th bursts to 45k.
- Cache hit baseline 22% -> 35% post-warmup; we should show both conservative and optimistic scenarios.
- Latency p95 target 700ms: given that, we should cap batching at ~8 concurrent tokens/batch in the hosted plan to avoid p95 regressions. For Dedicated we can show higher batching (16) with lower p95.

Ask for calculations in the model:
1) Effective tokens charged per call = tokens_total * (1 - cache_hit_rate) + tokens_for_response_always_counted. Show both raw and effective (post-cache).
2) Tokens per dollar table under three quantization profiles we support: FP16 (baseline), int8, and q4-ld. Include throughput delta and estimated price points.
3) Per-100k-tokens line for procurement + per-embedding line (they asked explicitly).

Timeline: Sara wants MAP to support Oct 15 close. We should turn around an updated cost sheet + short one-page MAP milestone list by Fri EOD PST so procurement gets it next week.

I'll draft the numbers if you can run the kernel/throughput sims and return per-token price estimates for each quant/profile. Also include a sensitivity row for prefix caching improvement from 22% -> 35%.

On the attachments: I'll merge their PM sheet into our cost model and attach an export with assumptions called pulse_cost_model_for_pulsehealth_v2.xlsx.

Thanks — Ben


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Sep 2025 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: unit-econ snapshot & eval plan - pilots to close timeline

Ben / Monica — ran the sims against the pilot profile Sara provided. High level results (summary to include in MAP page):

- Conservative (FP16, batch cap 8, cache 22%): effective tokens/call = 730 * (1 - 0.22) = ~569; projected tokens per $ = 1,250,000 (i.e., ~$0.0008 / token) => effective cost per request ≈ $0.45. Note: that number is for a single-hosted-engine price point; with volume discounts and reserved Dedicated pricing we can reduce token $ by ~3-4x.
- Optimistic (q4-ld, batch 16 on Dedicated, cache 35%): effective tokens/call ≈ 730 * (1 - 0.35) = ~475; tokens per $ ≈ 4,200,000 => effective cost per request ≈ $0.11. This aligns with the procurement line target if we combine Dedicated + reserved commit and caching improvements.

Attachments I'll produce: pulse_cost_model_for_pulsehealth_v2.xlsx (detailed calc), short one-pager 'map_milestones_for_pulse.pdf' draft.

Suggested MAP milestones to include in the email back to Pulse:
- 9/19: Redwood sends v2 cost model + 1-pager MAP milestones (assumptions table).
- 9/22: Joint eval run (3-day sampling) with Redwood tracing hooks enabled.
- 10/03: Procurement review & draft SOW.
- 10/10: Exec sign-off window.
- 10/15: Close and kick-off.

I'll attach the v2 model and the one-pager to the reply. If you want, I can also generate a short CSV of tokens-per-dollar sensitivity at different cache rates (22/28/35%) for procurement.

— Karthik
Staff ML Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

title: Clarifying data residency & deletion runbook for non-training assurance
preview:
From: Samir Chaudhry <samir@finhealth.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: FinHealth Security <security@finhealth.com>, procurement@finhealth.com
Date: Wed, 10 Sep 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick question on ‘no training’ claim + backups/replicas

Alex —

Thanks for the demo yesterday. Our security team needs a short write-up confirming two things before we proceed: 1) that Redwood does not use our tenant data to train any public or internal foundation models; and 2) exactly how long debug snapshots, backups, and cross-region replicas are retained after a tenant requests deletion.

Specifically: if FinHealth decides to stop using the service and requests full deletion, what happens to:
- KV caches/prefix caches used for inference performance;
- ephemeral debug snapshots captured for incident triage;
- backups and cross-region replicas (are they tombstoned immediately, or retained for a window?), and
- any telemetry that might be aggregated for platform improvement.

We need something concise we can attach to the procurement packet (1 page preferred).

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Chaudhry
Head of Security, FinHealth


From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Samir Chaudhry <samir@finhealth.com>
Cc: FinHealth Security <security@finhealth.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Sep 2025 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick question on ‘no training’ claim + backups/replicas

Samir —

Great questions. Short answer: we do not use customer tenant content to train our public foundation models. For the retention bullets you listed I want to loop in Mina (privacy/ops) to give exact windows and the deletion runbook steps.

I'll follow up with a one-page attestation plus a short runbook snippet that you can include in procurement. Expect a doc and a summary in the next 48 hours.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference


From: Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
To: Samir Chaudhry <samir@finhealth.com>, Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 11:23:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Quick question on ‘no training’ claim + backups/replicas

Hi Samir —

Thanks for the clear list. I outlined the operational behavior below; I'll attach the shorter attestation you requested and a matrix that maps each artifact to retention/erase behavior. If you want an even shorter single-sentence attestation for procurement, we can provide that as well.

Key points (operational detail):
- Training usage: Redwood does not feed tenant content into any general-purpose training pipeline for public or internal foundation models. We may use non-identifying, aggregated telemetry (e.g., request volume, error rates, latency histograms) to improve platform infrastructure; customers can opt out of telemetry aggregation per contract.

- KV cache / prefix cache: ephemeral caches are in-memory within the serving cluster and are invalidated on delete requests. For safety we mark the tenant data as deleted immediately and invalidate keys; any in-flight cache entries are evicted within the next maintenance window (normally < 24 hours).

- Debug snapshots / incident triage artifacts: we create snapshots only when debugging a specific issue. Those snapshots are tagged, access-controlled, and have a 30-day retention by default. On a tenant deletion request we run the delete-runbook: snapshots are moved to a tombstone state immediately and scheduled for irreversible purge after a 30-day retention window unless an active legal hold is raised. We can shorten the snapshot purging window contractually to 0 days for regulated customers (enterprise add-on).

- Backups & cross-region replicas: backups are retained for disaster recovery for a rolling 30-day period by default. Cross-region replicas are asynchronous and are considered part of backups for deletion purposes: when deletion is requested we trigger tombstone markers across all replica regions and schedule a purge job. Purge completes in a rolling process; typical completion is 30–45 days depending on dataset size and snapshot backlog. We provide attestations when purge completes.

- Telemetry and aggregated metrics: platform telemetry used for improving performance is generally aggregated and non-identifying. Customers who require zero telemetry retention can opt out; this is included as a line item in our Dedicated/Private contracts.

Actionable items we can provide you now: a one-page non-training attestation, the data retention matrix (attached), and a short delete-runbook summary (attached). If procurement needs the snapshots purge shortened we can propose add-on language for the SOW.

Regards,
Mina Haddad
Head of Privacy & Product Security
Redwood Inference


From: Markus Klein <markus@redwood.com>
To: Samir Chaudhry <samir@finhealth.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: FinHealth Security <security@finhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 08:49:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Quick question on ‘no training’ claim + backups/replicas

Samir / team —

Two legal clarifications to help procurement: 1) we can commit contractually that \"Redwood will not use Customer Content to train foundation models,\" and 2) retention windows (snapshots/backups) are operationally described as Mina noted. If FinHealth needs expedited purge (e.g., reduction from 30 days to 0 days), we require the SOW addendum and acknowledgement of increased operational risk for debugging/recovery.

I'll drop suggested redline language for the Master Services Agreement and a short addendum for the retention carveout into the HubSpot record. Alex — can you confirm the procurement contact so we route the DocuSign correctly?

—Markus
Markus Klein
VP Legal, Redwood Inference

- If procurement is happy with standard 30-day snapshot/backups purge window we will add the attestation and return the signed MSA with standard retention language.
