title: PII pseudonymization & on-prem access diagram review
preview:
From: Laura Simpson <laura@medicore.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: MediCore Security Team <security@medicore.com>
Date: 2025-09-15T09:22:11-07:00
Subject: PII pseudonymization & on-prem access diagram review

Hi Ava,

Thanks for taking this on. As part of MediCore's DPA annex review we're looking to confirm how Redwood will handle pseudonymization and access controls for our on-prem inference deployment. Specifically we need:

- A technical mapping of which fields you consider PII vs derived identifiers (name, MRN, device id, free-text notes).
- The pseudonymization approach (one-way hash, tokenization, reversible encryption) and where re-identification keys (if any) are stored.
- Network segmentation / access diagram showing which components are in-customer VPC vs Redwood-managed control plane, and where logs/audit trails are retained.
- Roles who can access re-identification (if applicable) and evidence of KMS/HSM integration.

Can you share a diagram and a short mapping spreadsheet by EOD Wednesday (9/17)? If there's anything you need from us (e.g., sample schema or classification tags) let me know.

Best,
Laura Simpson
Senior Privacy Counsel
MediCore Health



From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Laura Simpson <laura@medicore.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-09-15T11:08:34-07:00
Subject: Re: PII pseudonymization & on-prem access diagram review

Hi Laura,

Thanks — we can absolutely provide that. Attaching a working spreadsheet that maps the most common fields we see and our recommended pseudonymization pattern. High-level summary below; the attached draft has row-level notes per field.

Proposal (draft):
- Data classification: We classify explicit identifiers (name, MRN, SSN) as PII; device IDs and session tokens as persistent identifiers; free-text notes are treated as sensitive unstructured text.
- Pseudonymization approach: one-way salted hashing for identifiers used only for analytics (non-reversible); deterministic tokenization for cases where cross-session matching is required. Deterministic tokens are generated using a per-customer salt and a keyed tokenization service.
- Re-identification keys: for deterministic tokenization we do not store plaintext mapping in Redwood. Instead, the tokenization key is stored in the customer's KMS (recommended) or in an HSM-backed Redwood KMS instance for Dedicated/Private deployments. Redwood personnel do not have direct access to KMS plaintext without an approved CustOps playbook and two-person authorization.
- Access controls & audit: RBAC + scoped service accounts, hourly audit log export to customer-controlled S3, and event-level logging of any key unwrap operations.
- Network segmentation: for Private deployments, inference nodes sit in the customer VPC and only the agent/control-plane has outbound to Redwood for control messages. See attached network diagram (v1).

Attachments: draft-pseudonymization-mapping.xlsx (spreadsheet)

Next steps: Jonas — can you and Marissa review the proposed wording around tokenization, KMS custody, and the access approval flow? We'll incorporate any legal/security redlines before I send a cleaned version to MediCore.

Laura — can you confirm whether MediCore requires the re-identification key to be exclusively customer-managed KMS (preferred) or if a dedicated HSM instance managed by Redwood is acceptable subject to contract language?

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
(415) 555-0193



From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-09-16T10:12:47-07:00
Subject: Re: PII pseudonymization & on-prem access diagram review

Ava — quick technical review and a couple of suggested changes before this goes to the customer. Inline below.

On Sep 15, 2025, at 11:08 AM, Ava Chen wrote:
> Proposal (draft):
> - Data classification: We classify explicit identifiers (name, MRN, SSN) as PII; device IDs and session tokens as persistent identifiers; free-text notes are treated as sensitive unstructured text.

Suggestion: add a small note about derived features (e.g., embeddings) — treat as pseudonymized but include a retention TTL and forbid export without attestation.

> - Pseudonymization approach: one-way salted hashing for identifiers used only for analytics (non-reversible); deterministic tokenization for cases where cross-session matching is required. Deterministic tokens are generated using a per-customer salt and a keyed tokenization service.

Suggestion: for deterministic tokenization used in clinical matching, prefer format-preserving tokenization on input to reduce fingerprinting risk. Also call out rotation policy for salts/keys and mention a fallback strategy for token collisions.

> - Re-identification keys: for deterministic tokenization we do not store plaintext mapping in Redwood. Instead, the tokenization key is stored in the customer's KMS (recommended) or in an HSM-backed Redwood KMS instance for Dedicated/Private deployments. Redwood personnel do not have direct access to KMS plaintext without an approved CustOps playbook and two-person authorization.

Confirm: we should be explicit that CustOps approvals are recorded in an immutable audit trail and that any emergency unseal requires both a legal & security ticket. I've attached a redline of the network diagram (network-segmentation-v2.png) with a clearer depiction of the control-plane hop and the audit export path. Also adding a short KMS attestation template (kms-attestation-redacted.txt) that we can provide under NDA.

I'll also flag Marissa for any contractual language around 'exclusive customer KMS' vs 'Redwood HSM with contractual guarantees'.

Attachments: network-segmentation-v2.png, kms-attestation-redacted.txt

-Jonas
Jonas Weber
Principal Security Engineer, Redwood Inference
jonas.weber@redwood.com



From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Laura Simpson <laura@medicore.com>
Cc: MediCore Security Team <security@medicore.com>
Date: 2025-09-17T14:03:05-07:00
Subject: Re: PII pseudonymization & on-prem access diagram review

Hi Laura — attaching the consolidated materials and suggested contractual language excerpts per your ask. We've incorporated security feedback (see network segmentation v2) and outlined the custody options for re-identification keys. Summary:

1) Field mapping: attached spreadsheet maps common schema elements and our recommended transformation (one-way hash vs deterministic token). See spreadsheet rows for notes where free-text requires additional redaction.

2) Deterministic tokenization: supported for cross-session matching. Tokenization keys SHOULD be customer-managed in KMS. If MediCore prefers Redwood-managed HSM for Dedicated/Private, we can provide an HSM-backed key under a contractual attestation and two-person CustOps approval flow.

3) Access & audit: RBAC for service accounts, two-person approval for any key unseal, hourly audit export to customer-controlled storage, and immutable audit trail for approval events.

4) Network: inference nodes reside in the customer VPC. Control-plane communicates outbound only over mTLS to Redwood endpoints; audit exports flow to a customer S3 bucket in the same region (see network-segmentation-v2.png).

Attachments (for review):
- draft-pseudonymization-mapping.xlsx (spreadsheet)
- network-segmentation-v2.png (diagram)
- kms-attestation-redacted.txt (attestation template, redacted; full available under NDA)

Action items:
- Laura: please confirm whether exclusive customer KMS is required or if Redwood-managed HSM (with attestation) is acceptable.
- MediCore security: if you want any further detail on rotation policies or collision handling for deterministic tokens, tell us which artifacts (rotation cadence, deterministic algorithm spec) you prefer and we'll include.

If everything looks good we'll add the agreed language to the DPA annex and circulate to procurement for signature.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
(415) 555-0193

title: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness
preview:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 14:10:00 -0700
Subject: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned on procurement and billing ops: 

Summary from the call
- LedgerWave is excited about Redwood for the KYC/transaction enrichment pipeline.
- Procurement leans toward using a PO process for all 3rd-party SaaS purchases. They still evaluate marketplace options (AWS Marketplace) depending on discounts and SSO integration ease.

Open items for billing/procurement (we need confirmation from you):
1) Commercial path: PO (preferred) vs. card vs. marketplace — which will your procurement team route for this engagement?
2) Invoice requirements: billing address, VAT/tax ID, company legal name exactly as it should appear on invoices.
3) Net terms: do you require Net 30/45? Any internal approvals needed for Net terms?
4) Invoice format: PDF invoice (email), e-invoice/EDI, or custom fields required?
5) Remittance and purchase order workflow — do you need invoices to reference a PO number for payment to be processed?
6) Billing contact name + email for our Finance team to reach out.

Proposed next steps
- If you can attach your PO template and any billing-spec doc, I’ll loop in our Billing Ops (Karthik) to confirm we can produce invoices in the requested format.
- If procurement wants Marketplace, we can pull together a short comparison of expected differences (discounts, billing cadence, invoice fields).
- I’ll share a draft SOW/initial invoice example once we lock the path.

Would love your responses on the numbered items above — if easier, feel free to attach your PO template and billing requirements and I’ll coordinate internally.

Best,
Priya Natarajan
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
priya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: none in this message


From: Elena Sokolov <elena@ledgerwave.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, John Hayes <john.hayes@ledgerwave.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 17:02:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Commercial path + invoice preflight / procurement readiness

Hi Priya —

Thanks for the clear recap. Answers inline below and I attached the PO template + billing checklist for your team.

1) Commercial path: Procurement prefers PO-based purchases for anything over $10k. For smaller pilots they will accept company card, but for rollout we expect PO. They are open to AWS Marketplace if we can get the same billing detail and our legal team signs the marketplace terms.

2) Invoice requirements:
- Legal name: LedgerWave, Inc.
- Billing address: 2308 Harbor Drive, Suite 400, Boston, MA 02110
- VAT/Tax ID: US EIN 12-3456789 (we are US entity, no VAT).

3) Net terms: We prefer Net 30. For pilots they may pay on receipt if we provide PO + invoice with correct GL codes.

4) Invoice format: PDF emailed to ap@ledgerwave.com is fine. They also accept a structured XLS with line-level GL codes (I attached a sample spreadsheet). No EDI required at this stage.

5) Yes — invoice must reference the PO number. Payments won't be scheduled without a PO on the invoice.

6) Billing ops contact: John Hayes, Billing Ops Manager — john.hayes@ledgerwave.com, +1 (617) 555-1010.

Attached: LedgerWave_PO_template.pdf, LedgerWave_billing_requirements.xlsx

Let me know if you need anything else to get the initial SOW/invoice example over to us. We are aiming to have procurement sign-off within 2–3 weeks.

Best,
Elena
Director, Strategic Partnerships | LedgerWave Inc.
elena@ledgerwave.com

Head of Integrations, Clinix Health

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sam Taylor <sam.taylor@clinixhealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Sep 2025 08:33:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot onboarding timeline & gating checklist

Sam —

Thanks for the questions. I've sketched a mutual action plan (MAP) with owners and acceptance criteria and attached MAP-v1.docx. Highlights:

- We will provide a sandbox API key with IP allowlist by EOD Tuesday once we receive the dev mail list.
- Streaming: use response_mode=stream for low-latency chunks; we will enable prefix caching for repeated prompt fragments to reduce cost.
- Function/tool calling: we recommend idempotency tokens in the tool-call metadata (tool_call.idempotency_key) and a 'side_effects' boolean field in responses. Our runtime stores basic call logs; for full audit you can pull audit logs (retention 90 days by default, extendable via Dedicated/Private).

Docs & links are in the Drive folder; SOW for the pilot is ready for signature if you want to proceed after the kickoff (Docusign link below).

Proposed kickoff: Tue 16 Sep 2025 11:00 PT. Ben, can you confirm attendees?

Cheers,
Monica Patel
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

title: Pricing Exhibit: guardband mechanics and packaging handoff - redline proposal
preview:
From: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Rao <procurement@stratumio.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs

Hi Ava —

Thanks again for walking us through the demo. Procurement has run a first pass on the pricing exhibit and returned a few redlines that block internal approval. Their two core asks are: 1) clearer definition of the \"guardband\" / usage floor mechanics so it doesn't trigger unexpectedly during spikes, and 2) clearer language on how partner/marketplace discounts flow through to committed usage calculations.

I've attached the PDF with their markups (StratumIO_PricingExhibit_v3_redline.pdf) and a short table with example math. Can your team confirm:

- whether we can add a 90-day smoothing period for usage spikes before a floor true-up is assessed, and
- whether marketplace partner discounts are applied pre- or post-commit accounting.

Quick call would help — propose Tue 9/16 10am PT?

Thanks,
Jordan
StratumIO — Sr. Procurement Manager
[Attachment: StratumIO_PricingExhibit_v3_redline.pdf (PDF, 243KB)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Sep 2025 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs

Jordan —

Appreciate the detailed notes and the example table. We can do a 90-day smoothing window for true-ups, but legal wants that expressed as a \"smoothing period\" with an explicit maximum adjustment per quarter to avoid retroactive large reconciliations. Suggested language from our side:

- \"Smoothing period: Utilization variance will be assessed over a rolling 90-day window. True-up adjustments will not exceed 25% of the average quarterly committed volume in any single reconciliation.\"

On marketplace discounts: we typically apply partner discounts at the invoice line (post-rate) but count committed-equivalent usage on a pre-discount basis for the purposes of caps/floors so both parties have predictable commitments. That addresses revenue recognition complexity for buyers and avoids double-counting discounts.

Cc'ing Ben/Marissa/Karthik to review potential commercial concessions (credits/buffer) if Stratum needs a lower up-front guard. Also propose we capture the final guardband formula as an explicit table in the Order Schedule to avoid interpretation drift.

Happy to take the Tue 10am slot if that still works.

Best,
Ava Chen
Director, Sales — Redwood Inference
[Signature]


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Sep 2025 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: internal: proposed commercial concession for Stratum (guardband + packaging)

Team —

Read Jordan's notes and Ava's response. Commercially I can offer a one-quarter waiver of the floor for the first billing quarter (i.e., no floor true-up applied for Q1 post-commit), plus a temporary 10% onboarding credit to offset migration variance. This keeps revenue expectation but reduces the customer's risk during ramp.

Key tradeoffs:
- Waiver delays realization of committed revenue (internal impact on ARR smoothing).
- Credits should be capped and expire after 90 days.

If legal agrees to Ava's \"25% cap on adjustments per reconciliation\" language, this combo should be acceptable. I've attached a short spreadsheet with the concession math and an illustration of the invoice lines.

-Ben
Sr. Revenue Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: OrderSchedule_Summary_Table.xlsx (XLSX, 18KB)]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Sep 2025 11:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: internal: proposed commercial concession for Stratum (guardband + packaging)

All —

Legal edit suggestions inline to avoid ambiguity. Major items:

1) Define guardband trigger event explicitly: \"Guardband triggers when sustained utilization falls below 60% of committed monthly utilization measured over a 90-day rolling window (i.e., three consecutive 30-day periods).\"
2) Add an annual true-up cap: \"Annual reconciliation adjustments related to guardband true-ups shall not reduce billed amounts by more than 40% of the annual committed amount in any 12-month period.\"
3) Packaging/discount flow: \"Marketplace partner discounts shall be applied at invoice issuance; however, for the purpose of measuring compliance with committed usage, usage shall be measured on a gross basis (pre-discount).\"

Marissa suggests replacing the term \"smoothing period\" with \"rolling window\" to match standard contract language. Laura — please review the annual cap number; I set 40% as a negotiation anchor but open to lowering.

I'll convert these points to tracked changes in the attached PDF and push back if procurement asks for a full removal of guardband.

-Marissa
Head of Legal Operations, Redwood Inference


From: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@stratumio.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Rao <procurement@stratumio.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Sep 2025 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pricing exhibit redline & packaging Qs — call notes and next steps

Ava/Marissa/Ben —

Thanks for the call earlier. Quick recap and next steps from Stratum:

- The 90-day rolling window + 25% per-reconciliation cap (as described by Ava) is a workable starting point for our CFO, but they asked to replace the 25% cap with a graduated matrix: 0–30 days = 0% adjustment, 31–90 days = up to 25%, >90 days = up to 40% (they want stronger protection for multi-quarter underutilization).
- On discounts, confirmed Stratum prefers counting committed usage on a net basis (post-discount) because partner rebates flow through the billing provider and affect cash flow. They asked if Redwood can run a middle-ground: count pre-discount for the cap/floor math but offer a quarterly reconciliation credit equal to the delta between pre- and post-discount accounting up to a capped amount.

Action items we agreed on:
1) Redwood to send a redline PDF reflecting Marissa's suggested legal edits plus two commercial concession options (Ben's Q1 waiver + onboarding credit vs. a graduated matrix alternative).
2) Stratum to circulate the revised redline to finance and return final position by 9/25.
3) Redwood to prepare a short one-page example worksheet that shows how pre/post discount counting and the quarterly delta-credit would work in practice.

Jordan — please confirm receipt and I will roll these edits into the DocuSign test envelope once we lock the language.

Best,
Jordan
Sr. Procurement Manager, StratumIO
[Attachment referenced: StratumIO_PricingExhibit_v3_redline.pdf]

title: Integrator sandbox + Train-the-Trainer plan for BlueMap Consulting
preview:
From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@bluemap.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-09-03T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Partner onboarding: Integrator enablement and sandbox access

Hi Avery,

Thanks for the quick connect last week — following up from our call. BlueMap is onboarding a small systems integrator arm (8 engineers, 2 delivery leads) that will be responsible for integrating Redwood into three customer pilots over the next quarter. We want a structured enablement path so the SI can certify internally and then deliver repeatable implementations.

title: SLA uplift request & monthly credit reconciliation
preview:
From: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Sep 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Hi Ava / Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — Peregrine is excited about Redwood's latency profile for our inference-heavy pipelines. Before we move into drafting an amendment, procurement asked for two clarifications that will affect the commercial ask:

1) Uptime target: we need a firm commitment for the hosted tier at 99.95% (monthly availability) given our SLA with downstream customers. Is that achievable for your hosted plan or would that require Dedicated capacity?

2) Monthly credit logic: we want the credit calculation to scale proportionally across partial-month degradations rather than a binary trigger. I attached a brief spreadsheet with our expected call volumes and a reference formula. Can Redwood propose a simple table that maps availability bands to credit percentages (monthly credit as % of invoice)?

If possible, could you share a draft amendment and a worked example showing how credits would be calculated for a 3% degradation in p95 latency vs a 0.2% uptime shortfall? Procurement will need the exact wording for their internal approval.

Thanks — Emily
Emily Russo
Head of Procurement, Peregrine Analytics
Attachments: Credit_Calc_Sheet.xlsx


From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Sep 2025 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Hi Emily — thanks for the pragmatic notes and for the spreadsheet, that helped. Quick answers up front:

- Hosted tier: our standard hosted SLA is 99.9% availability. 99.95% is above our standard hosted commitment; we can either (a) offer a dedicated capacity commitment at 99.95% under a reserved contract, or (b) for hosted, we can propose a 99.92% uplift with a small operational exception window and a higher credit band if you prefer to remain on hosted.

- Monthly credit logic: we typically use banded crediting (e.g., >=99.9% -> 0; 99.5–99.9% -> X%; <99.5% -> Y%). However, I can translate that into a proportional formula for your procurement team — the attached draft shows both banded and proportional examples and two worked scenarios (p95 latency regression vs uptime shortfall).

I attached an initial amendment (SLA_Amendment_Peregrine_v0.1.docx) and the credit calc we discussed. Can Marissa/Laura confirm whether we should start with the Dedicated 99.95% path or propose the hosted uplift with higher credit exposure?

Action items:
- Emily: confirm preference (Dedicated vs hosted uplift) and whether the credit should be expressed as % of monthly invoice or as days-of-service equivalent.
- Marissa: I'll need a short note on commercial delta to provision a dedicated pool if they choose it.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
Attachments: SLA_Amendment_Peregrine_v0.1.docx; Credit_Calc_Sheet.xlsx


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Sep 2025 10:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Emily — looping in Laura (legal) and putting in a concrete proposal to keep the procurement review moving.

Proposal summary (redwood):
- Option A: Dedicated commitment (reserved pool) — 99.95% monthly uptime. Remedy: banded credits: 99.90–99.95% -> 10% of monthly invoice; 99.00–99.90% -> 30%; <99.00% -> 60%. This requires an annual reserved commitment and a short lead time to provision.

- Option B: Hosted uplift pilot — 99.92% monthly uptime (90-day pilot). Remedy: proportional credits expressed as a percentage of the monthly invoice using the attached formula (see sheet) with a cap of 50% per month. Because this remains on shared capacity, it includes an operational exception for scheduled maintenance windows and limited burst events.

We propose expressing credits as % of invoice (simpler for procurement). The attached SLA draft uses clear band definitions and an illustrative worked example for a 3% p95 latency regression (translated into equivalent credit using the latency-to-availability mapping in the doc).

Legal note (Laura): please see section 4.2 in the draft for the exact credit computation; if that looks good, I can send this version to your procurement team and request a short turn on redlines.

Marissa Cole
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference
Attachments: SLA_Amendment_Peregrine_v0.1.docx; Credit_Calc_Sheet.xlsx

>
> - Monthly credit logic: we typically use banded crediting (e.g., >=99.9% -> 0; 99.5–99.9% -> X%; <99.5% -> Y%). However, I can translate that into a proportional formula for your procurement team — the attached draft shows both banded and proportional examples and two worked scenarios (p95 latency regression vs uptime shortfall).
>
> Ava


From: Emily Russo <erusso@peregrineanalytics.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Sep 2025 16:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Initial ask: uptime target and monthly credit calc

Thanks — appreciate the clear options. Procurement has sign-off authority for pilot pilots but the board will only approve an annual reserved commitment if we get 99.95% on Dedicated. For now we want to: (1) proceed with Option B hosted uplift pilot at 99.92% expressed as % of invoice with the 50% cap, and (2) add a condition that, after a successful 90-day pilot (defined as <0.1% availability incidents), we convert to a Dedicated 99.95% commitment with a 6-month provisioning lead and a 12-month reserved term.

I reviewed the amendment and suggested two minor redline points in the shared doc (doc comments): clarifying scheduled maintenance notification windows and adding a Customer contact escalation matrix. Once you accept those minor edits we'll loop in Finance for the commercial delta.

Emily

> Emily — looping in Laura (legal) and putting in a concrete proposal to keep the procurement review moving.
>
> Proposal summary (redwood):
> - Option A: Dedicated commitment (reserved pool) — 99.95% monthly uptime. Remedy: banded credits: 99.90–99.95% -> 10% of monthly invoice; 99.00–99.90% -> 30%; <99.00% -> 60%. This requires an annual reserved commitment and a short lead time to provision.
>
> - Option B: Hosted uplift pilot — 99.92% monthly uptime (90-day pilot). Remedy: proportional credits expressed as a percentage of the monthly invoice using the attached formula (see sheet) with a cap of 50% per month. Because this remains on shared capacity, it includes an operational exception for scheduled maintenance windows and limited burst events.
>
> Thanks,
> Marissa

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@lumina-fin.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Aug 2025 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Lumina - next steps: sizing call + demo cadence

Hi Alex — thanks again for carving out time today. Quick recap from our discovery and three proposed next steps so we can lock a predictable path for evaluation:

Summary from call (high level)
- Primary workload: real-time document ingestion + semantic search + chat assistance for customer support workflows.
- Target QPS: 60 peak requests/s (in bursts), baseline 10 req/s.
- Latency SLO: 95th percentile < 250ms for short prompts (~256 tokens); longer context paths acceptable for async batch.
- Data requirements: PII redaction at ingest, retention policy 90 days; will need private deployment or dedicated pool with strict access controls.

Suggested next steps
1) Dedicated capacity sizing call (technical deep dive): validate load profile, tokenization characteristics, KV cache considerations, and fallback policy. Estimated 60–80 GPU-hours/week if using optimized quantized model family; we’ll refine after the deep dive.
2) Short demo (30 min): show prefix caching, batching knobs, and cost/lantency trade-offs with a live workload sample.
3) Draft mutual action plan + timeline: procurement windows and trial period.

Proposed slots for the sizing call (times in PDT; converting to EDT / BST on request):
- Mon Aug 25: 10:00–11:00am PDT / 1:00–2:00pm EDT
- Tue Aug 26: 8:00–9:00am PDT / 11:00–12:00pm EDT
- Wed Aug 27: 3:00–4:00pm PDT / 6:00–7:00pm EDT

Please reply with your preferred slot and any additional folks we should include (ops/security/procurement). I’ve attached a short capacity checklist and a one-pager demo brief to help you prep.

Thanks — I’ll coordinate calendar invites once we confirm.

Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference.com | (415) 555-0123
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>

Subject: Winfield Health — go-live logistics, owner assignment & tactical checklist

Hi team,

Quick handoff: Winfield Health (see Sarah CC'd) signed the SOW yesterday and want to target a staged go-live for 2025-09-15 (pilot cohort of 250 clinicians). Deal details below and a short set of asks so we can kick the post-sign playbook into motion.

Deal summary (short):
- Contract: 12 months, SOW (attached) signed — platform + 3 integrations.
- Initial scope: clinician triage assistant + search embeddings.
- Committed throughput: 120 rps peak for pilot, expected steady-state 30 rps.
- Special terms: 30-day rollback window; Redwood to provide one-week dedicated onboarding support.

Primary customer contacts:
- Sarah Thompson, Dir. of Product Ops — sarah.thompson@winfieldhealth.com (CC)
- They will nominate an infra lead for network whitelisting shortly.

Immediate asks for CS/Onboarding (please confirm owners):
1) Owner assignment for technical kickoff & runbook ownership (we need a single point of contact).
2) Schedule a 60-min kickoff with Winfield + infra (target week of 2025-09-01).
3) Confirm checklist: network allowlist, API keys, sandbox creds, initial prompt set, and evaluation metrics.
4) Review the attached MutualActionPlan_Winfield_v3.xlsx — it has proposed milestones.

Notes: They asked for a minor custom mapping on our events schema (field: patient_id_hash) — flagged legal/compliance already; will require a short tech design call.

Ben

ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

title: Greenforge supplier invite failing at CAPTCHA/step-up
preview:
From: Aaron Patel <a.patel@greenforge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kara Liu <kara.liu@greenforge.com>
Date: Thu, 21 Aug 2025 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Greenforge onboarding — invite stuck on CAPTCHA/step-up

Hi Ben / Neha,

We're seeing multiple supplier reps fail to accept the Coupa invite from Greenforge. The flow gets to the CAPTCHA/step-up page right after the SSO redirect and then returns a generic 403 or the page just reloads. I attached a screenshot from one of the suppliers (captcha-screenshot.png).

This is blocking PO submission for an urgent order (PO #GF-8819) and procurement is on a tight deadline. Can you confirm whether this is an issue on your end or if it's something our suppliers are doing?

- Example supplier: Trident Packaging (user: jenna@tridentpack.com)
- Browser: Chrome 115 on macOS
- Tried incognito and same result

Appreciate a quick look — we can hop on a call if helpful.

Thanks,
Aaron
Aaron Patel
Head of Procurement Ops
Greenforge Solutions


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aaron Patel <a.patel@greenforge.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 21 Aug 2025 15:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Greenforge onboarding — invite stuck on CAPTCHA/step-up

Hi Aaron — thanks for flagging and for the screenshot.

Short answer: we’ve seen a few similar reports recently that line up with Akamai/edge bot challenges firing mid-flow. Two quick asks so we can triage fast:
1) Can you confirm the exact invite link (if you’re comfortable pasting the URL) or the tenant id from the invite?
2) Any additional screenshot of the browser console/network tab would help but not required.

Immediate actions from our side: I’ve looped in Neha (Identity & Integrations) and Lauren (Onboarding Ops). Neha, can you open an infra ticket and check whether our challenge TTLs are being triggered for the Coupa redirect path? Lauren — I’ll need a formal support ticket created with Greenforge as the customer so we can escalate to edge networking if needed.

We can also push a temporary workaround: reissue a direct activation magic link that bypasses the redirect challenge for the affected users. That’s not ideal long-term but could unblock the PO today. Aaron — would you like us to generate a couple of one-time links for Trident and any other impacted suppliers?

I’ll follow up once Neha confirms.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Strategic Procurement
Redwood Inference


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aaron Patel <a.patel@greenforge.com>
Date: Thu, 21 Aug 2025 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Greenforge onboarding — invite stuck on CAPTCHA/step-up

On Thu, 21 Aug 2025 at 15:02, Aaron Patel <a.patel@greenforge.com> wrote:
> We're seeing multiple supplier reps fail to accept the Coupa invite ... CAPTCHA/step-up page ... returns a generic 403

Thanks for the details, Aaron — adding the technical notes here so the infra team has context. I attached a dump of headers from a failing invite (invite-headers.txt) and a small zipped log set (greenforge_onboard_log.zip).

Symptom summary (internal):
- Bot/challenge triggers after SSO relay redirect: edge service flags the session as risky when the request arrives with missing/blocked third-party cookies or a stale session cookie.
- The challenge page injects a JS step-up; if the browser blocks the challenge (common on some corporate or privacy plugins), the edge returns a 403 instead of completing the flow.
- We’ve reproduced with vendor accounts behind certain corporate proxies that strip or rewrite X-Forwarded-For.

Suggested fixes (short-term -> long-term):
1) Short-term workaround: issue one-time activation links that include a signed tenant token (bypass the redirect path). Ops can generate these; they expire in 6 hours.
2) Mid-term: add an allowlist rule for known Greenforge edge IP ranges/XFF prefixes while we investigate the bot rules.
3) Long-term: adjust challenge TTL and ensure the relaystate persists across the SSO handoff for Coupa? This requires an infra config change to session cookie path and a small change in the relaystate handler.

Action items:
- Neha: open RED-4521 on the infra board (I'll do this now), attach logs and sample XFFs.
- Lauren: request Greenforge supplier list for affected users; if they provide emails we will pre-warm one-time links.
- Ben: confirm whether we should generate one-time links for Trident+any others.

Neha Kapoor
Identity & Integrations Engineer
Redwood Inference


From: Kara Liu <kara.liu@greenforge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aaron Patel <a.patel@greenforge.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Aug 2025 08:07:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Greenforge onboarding — invite stuck on CAPTCHA/step-up

On Thu, 21 Aug 2025 at 16:04, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Symptom summary (internal): ...

Thanks — attaching an additional screenshot and the supplier email list (included in the drive link).

Impacted suppliers so far: Trident Packaging (jenna@tridentpack.com), Halcyon Components (mike@halcyon.com), Meridian Labels (alisha@meridianlabels.com). All reported identical behavior (challenge shows, then 403 or endless reload).

We're under a deadline on their PO approvals this afternoon — if you can generate one-time links for the three users above that would unblock the first set. Also able to join a quick call if it helps to validate changes. ETA request: can you provide an estimate for a permanent fix?

Thanks,
Kara Liu
Senior Vendor Ops
Greenforge Solutions

You can track progress here: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/RED-4521.

Short ETA:
- One-time links: rolling now, should be delivered within 20 minutes.
- Allowlist/mid-term mitigation: if Greenforge can provide IP ranges for us to whitelist, we can push a temporary allowlist within 1 business day.
- Permanent fix (relaystate/session TTL): estimated 2-3 sprints, depends on infra prioritization. I’ll escalate to the infra manager for SLA consideration given your deadline.

title: Activation certification & call-tree evidence request
preview:
From: Lydia Park <lpark@harborbank.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops@harborbank.com
Date: Mon, 18 Aug 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Evidence request — activation certification & call-tree

Hi Camila,

Thanks again for the walkthrough last week. Our audit team has one final ask before we close the resilience section for the vendor review: can you provide a compact evidence packet that demonstrates actual activation of your warm-standby during a simulated failover, plus the on-call call-tree that was used during that exercise? Specifically we need:

- An activation log with timestamps showing the failover trigger, cache warm-up start, and service restore milestones (RTO/RPO relevant).
- The on-call roster and escalation path that was exercised (names, roles, contact order).
- Any short trace excerpts showing KV cache restores tied to the exercise.

If you can bundle these as attachments or point us at a short Drive folder, that would be ideal. We hope to have this by EOD Wednesday (20 Aug) for the auditor.

Best,
Lydia
Lydia Park
Sr. Vendor Risk Analyst — HarborBank


From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Lydia Park <lpark@harborbank.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 18 Aug 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evidence request — activation certification & call-tree

Hi Lydia,

Thanks — we can absolutely provide that. Quick plan and what to expect:

1) We ran a warm-standby activation tabletop on 2025-07-30 and a live warm-start drill on 2025-08-14. Marcus will extract the activation timestamps and KV-cache warm-up markers; that will show the RTO and RPO slices.

2) Attached is the oncall_roster_v2.csv (redacted phone numbers) and a short warm_start_activation_log_20250819.txt that contains the event timeline from our 08/14 drill (timestamps are UTC).

3) Ben has pulled a minimal failover_trace_excerpt.pdf with the relevant RPC traces — those are excerpts, not full logs, to avoid PII.

4) I'll also share a Drive folder link for the auditor. If HarborBank's team needs signed attestation, we can route via DocuSign (I included a placeholder link).

We're aiming to get everything to you by Tuesday EOD your time so you have a day to ingest before the Wednesday cutoff. If you need stricter RTO/RPO tables (per-route latencies), tell me which service endpoints to prioritize and Ben/Marcus will prepare a short table.

Camila Reyes
Senior Security & Compliance Program Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments: oncall_roster_v2.csv, warm_start_activation_log_20250819.txt, failover_trace_excerpt.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>, Lydia Park <lpark@harborbank.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 19 Aug 2025 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evidence request — activation certification & call-tree

Lydia / team,

Attaching the three artifacts Camila mentioned — small notes inline below.

--- warm_start_activation_log_20250819.txt (excerpt) ---
2025-08-14T02:01:12Z - DR_RUN_TRIGGERED - operator: sim-failover-001
2025-08-14T02:01:20Z - KV_CACHE_WARMUP_START - service: inference-gateway
2025-08-14T02:01:57Z - SERVICE_HEALTHY - inference-gateway (accepting requests)
2025-08-14T02:02:10Z - BACKEND_SYNC_COMPLETE - datastore-primary -> standby-replica
Estimated RTO observed: 65 seconds from trigger to accepting requests (95th pct).
Estimated RPO observed: < 2s (cross-check: replication lag sampling).

Notes: timestamps are UTC and aligned to our internal NTP. We redact operator IDs for privacy but can provide a signed attestation that these events were observed by the run coordinator.

--- oncall_roster_v2.csv ---
Columns: role, name, email, escalation_order, primary_contact_type
Samples:
\"SRE Lead\", \"Marcus Lin\", \"marcus_lin@redwood.ai\", \"1\", \"pager\"
\"DR Coordinator\", \"Camila Reyes\", \"camila_reyes@redwood.ai\", \"2\", \"phone\"
\"Ops Backup\", \"Hannah Schmitt\", \"hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai\", \"3\", \"email\"

We removed direct mobile numbers but include PagerDuty IDs in the Drive bundle.

--- failover_trace_excerpt.pdf ---
This is a 2-page extract showing the RPC handoff and a condensed KV fetch timeline correlated to the activation timestamps. Not full traces but sufficient for auditor review.

If HarborBank requires an observed witness (live call) next week, we can schedule a 20-minute demo and run coordinator commentary.

Ben Carter
Principal Site Reliability Engineer
Redwood Inference


From: Lydia Park <lpark@harborbank.com>

Cc: ops@harborbank.com
Date: Wed, 20 Aug 2025 16:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evidence request — activation certification & call-tree

1) Please confirm the timezone used in the activation log (you mentioned UTC; please add the UTC note to the first line).

title: RFI: Dataflow / PHI handling questions for St George's Health
preview:
From: Claire Moss <claire.moss@stgeorgeshealth.org>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 12 Aug 2025 09:15:00 -0400
Subject: RFI: Dataflow / PHI handling questions

Ben —

Thanks for taking this RFI. St George's is consolidating vendor responses for our upcoming vendor review. We have a short set of technical questions around PHI handling that we need answered before the 20 Aug review meeting. Can you confirm the points below and return any supporting evidence (diagrams, config snippets, SOC2 report references, or short screenshots)? A single PDF or ZIP is fine.

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 14 Aug 2025 10:45:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: RFI: Dataflow / PHI handling questions — technical appendix

Ben / Neha —

Draft technical appendix below (I will drop the diagram and config snippets into the shared drive and attach the PNG in the final PDF):

-- Technical appendix (answers to Claire's numbered items) --
1) PHI processing: Hosted customers can process PHI only after a signed BAA and when tenant isolation/contracted settings are enabled. For healthcare enterprise customers we recommend Dedicated or Private deployments. Model variants: standard Redwood-verified open models and quantized variants compatible with the customer's isolation level; model weights never stored in customer data buckets.

2) Encryption: Transport — TLS 1.3 with mutual TLS option for Dedicated/Private endpoints. At rest — AES-256 envelope encryption. Hosted: Redwood-managed CMKs (AWS KMS) with cross-account encryption keys, HSM-backed key material. Private: customer-managed KMS (AWS KMS, GCP KMS, Azure KeyVault) or on-prem HSM (FIPS 140-2 Level 3 supported). Key rotation — data keys rotated every 90 days by default; master key rotation cadence configurable per-customer (we propose annual rotation).

3) Dataflow: Inference requests from the client enter the control plane (ingress) over TLS, are routed to inference nodes for execution, and ephemeral request buffers are kept in memory on the GPU host; no raw request logs are written to persistent object storage unless the customer enables logging. KV/prefix cache (LLM runtime cache) is disabled by default for PHI customers; when enabled, it is scoped to tenant-specific memory-backed caches on the inference host and not persisted to durable object stores. Diagram: redwood_dataflow_diagram.png (attached).

4) Retention & deletion: Default telemetry-only retention (aggregate metrics) retained 24 months; request-level content retention is 0 days by default (no persistent storage); when customers enable logging for debugging, retention is configurable with a minimum of 30 days. Deletion API: customers can call the deletion endpoint (DELETE /v1/customers/{id}/requests) which triggers deletion across control plane indices and any durable stores. Proof: we provide deletion job receipts and a redacted deletion log suitable for auditors.

5) Audit & monitoring: All access and admin actions are logged to an append-only audit stream with SHA-256 hashes and stored in an audit logging bucket. Hosted default retention is 12 months; Dedicated/Private options available for longer retention and export to customer SIEM (Syslog/Splunk/Datadog). Access to logs is RBAC-restricted and requires least-privilege IAM roles; we can provide sample IAM bindings.

I'll drop config snippets and the diagram into the shared drive path and feed the artifacts to Ben for the final PDF.

- Priyom
Senior Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Olga Petrov <olga_petrov@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-08-13T09:21:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: RE: Initial: Order form + Publicity / logo questions — ClearSky wants embargo + 5-day approval

Ben — quick legal take and recommended compromise below. Key constraints: (1) we need the ability to reference customers for sales/marketing; (2) we cannot permit unbounded use of logos/metrics without prior review; (3) we should avoid operationally tight approval SLAs that could create silent holds on go-to-market activities.

Suggested approach (redline language you can send to Ellie):
"Mutual Publicity and References: Except as required by applicable law, neither party shall issue any press release or make any public announcement regarding the existence of the relationship without the other party's prior written consent. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the parties may include each other's company name in lists of customers and short-form marketing materials (\"Customer List\") without prior written consent, provided that no logos, customer-specific performance metrics, or testimonials shall be used without prior written approval. Where prior approval is required for logo or testimonial use, the approving party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to respond within five (5) business days of receipt of a written request. For clarity, approval shall not be unreasonably withheld; if no response is provided within ten (10) business days, approval shall be deemed denied and the parties shall revert to using a marketing-only name/reference until approval is granted."

Rationale: this gives marketing what they need (Customer List usage) while forcing a reasonable cadence for logo/testimonial approvals. We added an explicit fallback (deemed denied after ten days) to avoid open-ended hold-ups. If they accept, we can proceed to sign the order form this week and finalize longer-form testimonial language post-signature.

Other notes: Rachel — confirm we are OK with the "deemed denied after ten days" fallback operationally? Marcus — any procurement red flags on the 10-day clause? Olga — any privacy/data concerns with the anonymized metrics carveout they requested?

If everyone signs off, Ben, send this to Ellie and propose signing by 8/15 with the fallback language included in the order form.

— Marissa
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.com

They said they'd accept a temporary "marketing-only name/reference" with no logo/metrics if language can't be finalized by signing.

Questions:
- Can we accept the 5 business day approval window?
- Is a temporary reference (company name only) acceptable from a risk perspective?
- If we insist on a narrower window, what language would we propose to get them to sign this week?

Context: this customer will be a marquee reference for our analytics integrations; marketing/product want to keep the option for a short case study live soon after launch. I don't want to overpromise public quotes. Appreciate an actionable set of options I can share with Ellie.

Thanks — Ben

On 2025-08-12 13:45, Ellie Harper wrote:
> Ben — thanks for sending this over so quickly. Looping in our legal/business ops (Dana) per your ask. A couple quick notes from our side: ...
>

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Olga Petrov <olga_petrov@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-08-13T09:21:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: RE: Initial: Order form + Publicity / logo questions — ClearSky wants embargo + 5-day approval

Ben — quick legal take and recommended compromise below. Key constraints: (1) we need the ability to reference customers for sales/marketing; (2) we cannot permit unbounded use of logos/metrics without prior review; (3) we should avoid operationally tight approval SLAs that could create silent holds on go-to-market activities.

Suggested approach (redline language you can send to Ellie):
"Mutual Publicity and References: Except as required by applicable law, neither party shall issue any press release or make any public announcement regarding the existence of the relationship without the other party's prior written consent. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the parties may include each other's company name in lists of customers and short-form marketing materials (\"Customer List\") without prior written consent, provided that no logos, customer-specific performance metrics, or testimonials shall be used without prior written approval. Where prior approval is required for logo or testimonial use, the approving party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to respond within five (5) business days of receipt of a written request. For clarity, approval shall not be unreasonably withheld; if no response is provided within ten (10) business days, approval shall be deemed denied and the parties shall revert to using a marketing-only name/reference until approval is granted."

Rationale: this gives marketing what they need (Customer List usage) while forcing a reasonable cadence for logo/testimonial approvals. We added an explicit fallback (deemed denied after ten days) to avoid open-ended hold-ups. If they accept, we can proceed to sign the order form this week and finalize longer-form testimonial language post-signature.

Other notes: Rachel — confirm we are OK with the "deemed denied after ten days" fallback operationally? Marcus — any procurement red flags on the 10-day clause? Olga — any privacy/data concerns with the anonymized metrics carveout they requested?

If everyone signs off, Ben, send this to Ellie and propose signing by 8/15 with the fallback language included in the order form.

— Marissa
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.com

title: POC traffic charter and runway — post-call recapping workload profile
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Shaw <alex.shaw@solsticehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 09 Aug 2025 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: POC traffic charter and runway — post-call recapping workload profile

Alex / team — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick write-up to capture what we agreed and the immediate next steps so nothing falls through the gaps. I pulled this together with Marissa and Karthik on the line: 

Summary (what success looks like)
- Validate Redwood provides 95th-percentile serving latency <= 250ms for typical queries at the committed baseline load.
- Confirm cost-per-1k-tokens estimate within 15% of our forecast when using the recommended batching/quantization profile.
- No functional regressions on top-3 prompt families defined below.

Evaluation plan (how we'll judge the POC)
1) Handshake week (this week): ingest sample payloads, validate tokenization and prefix caching behavior.
2) Baseline run: sustained 300 RPS for 1 hour using avg prompt length 120 tokens / avg completion 80 tokens, concurrency target 40.
3) Burst probe: 1-minute bursts peaking at 2,400 RPS with the same token mix to exercise autoscaling/burst capacity.
4) Regression checks: run 200 labeled prompts through our eval harness; track pass/fail and look for drop in quality > 5%.

Workload details we captured from your call (please confirm):
- Typical RPS: 100–350 (baseline 300)
- Concurrency: 20–50 (target test 40)
- Prompt lengths: median 95 tokens, 75th pct 140 tokens
- Generated completion lengths: median 60 tokens, tail up to 400 for long-form responses
- Burst patterns: two daily scheduled spikes (morning + evening) and unpredictable marketing-driven bursts lasting 45–90s

Ownership and timelines
- Data ingestion + tokenization validation (owner: Marissa) — complete by 2025-08-12
- Baseline run + metrics collection (owner: Karthik) — run by 2025-08-14
- Cost/runbook assessment (owner: Ben) — readout 2025-08-15

Attachments: see poc_runway_sheet.xlsx (runway + checkpoints), sample_request_payloads.jsonl (one week of anonymized traffic), and Redwood security one-pager.

Immediate asks for Alex/Solstice:
- Confirm the sample S3 path and whether we have a short-lived IAM role or pre-signed URLs.
- Confirm which three prompt families you want used for the regression checks (you mentioned: triage, patient-summary, billing-extract).
- Agree to the baseline run window (we suggested 2025-08-14 09:00–11:00 PDT).

Next sync: 30-minute readiness call Tuesday 2025-08-12 10:00 PDT (tentative — I’ll send a calendar invite once you confirm).

Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference

--
Attachments referenced: poc_runway_sheet.xlsx (xlsx), sample_request_payloads.jsonl (jsonl), redwood_security_one-pager.pdf (pdf)

title: Recap and technical scope from today's Nova Health integration call
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: María González <maria.gonzalez@novahealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Aug 2025 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Recap and technical scope from today's Nova Health integration call

Hi María —

Thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our side to make sure we captured priorities and next steps. Please reply if anything below needs correction.

High-level pain points you shared:
- Current clinical search is noisy: clinicians are getting irrelevant hits and slow response times for note search (avg latency ~800ms).
- Integration into your EHR requires structured outputs (JSON payloads) to populate discrete fields, not just free text.
- Security & data residency: logs/audit trail and VPC-only connectivity are required for production.
- Cost predictability: you want to avoid unexpected per-token spikes as usage grows.

Primary goals discussed:
1) Deploy a proof-of-concept to power semantic search + reranking for 6k monthly active clinicians (start small, scale later).
2) Deliver structured extraction for discharge summaries (function-calling / structured outputs).
3) Achieve sub-300ms p95 latency for single-turn search queries and live streaming for clinician assistants.
4) Validate security posture (SOC2 + VPC private deployment) and procurement terms.

Product fit / integration notes we covered:
- Embeddings + reranking: use Redwood embeddings for vector search; we recommend approximate nearest neighbor with a reranker endpoint to improve precision on top-K results.
- Streaming: we can stream token-by-token for the assistant UI; SDK supports chunked responses and client-side reassembly.
- Function calling / structured outputs: we sketched a JSON schema for discharge-summary fields. Redwood's function-calling model can return typed JSON and strict enums to minimize downstream parsing errors.
- SDK & infra: quickstart SDK changes are minimal — you can switch to streaming calls with a single flag; we've included examples in the attached prototype snippets.

Proposed next steps (agreed on call):
- Technical deep-dive (1 hour) with your engineering + ours (Dev + Laura). Proposed slots: Thu 8/14 10am PT or Fri 8/15 11am PT.
- Provide a security checklist and SOC2 evidence pack in parallel (we'll send the docs by EOD tomorrow).
- Share a scoped POC SOW and estimate for a 6-week pilot (includes dedicated test dataset, model config, and monitoring).

Attachments: redwood-nova-ma-demo-recording.mp4 (call recording), prototype-sdk-snippets.zip (sample code for streaming + function calling).

Open items for you:
- Confirm the proposed deep-dive slot that works for your team.
- Confirm which EHR system endpoints we should aim at (FHIR-based API or custom endpoints).

Thanks — looking forward to the deep-dive.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0187

----
Attachment: prototype-sdk-snippets.zip (6 files)


From: María González <maria.gonzalez@novahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: NOVA Health Procurement <procurement@novahealth.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Aug 2025 17:42:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Recap and technical scope from today's Nova Health integration call

Hi Ben —

This is perfect, thanks for the clear write-up — confirms everything we discussed. A few clarifications from our side:

- EHR endpoint: we're FHIR-native (Epic instance) but have a middleware layer that accepts JSON payloads. Best target for POC is the middleware API (we'll share endpoint details in the deep-dive).
- Security: procurement will want to see the SOC2 report + an architecture diagram showing VPC peering and key management.
- Pilot acceptance criteria: clinically relevant top-5 precision ≥ 0.75 on our baseline queries, and structured extraction F1 ≥ 0.80 for the discharge-summary fields.

Procurement (cc'd) — we'll need a short commercial summary and a PO-ready quote after the SOW. Can Redwood provide a 6-week pilot SOW + itemized estimate?

Best,
María González
Director of Clinical Informatics, NOVA Health
maría.gonzalez@novahealth.com | +1 617-555-0124

On Tue, Aug 12, 2025 at 2:05 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> [Quoted earlier email omitted]

----


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: María González <maria.gonzalez@novahealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, NOVA Health Procurement <procurement@novahealth.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Aug 2025 18:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap and technical scope from today's Nova Health integration call

María —

Great, we'll proceed exactly as you outlined. Quick answers to procurement / acceptance criteria:

- SOW & quote: attaching a short draft SOW for the 6-week pilot and a high-level cost estimate. If procurement needs an adjusted billing cadence we can adapt (monthly vs milestone).
- SOC2 + architecture: Laura will send the SOC2 snapshot and an architecture diagram showing how Redwood Private can be deployed in a VPC with KMS integration.
- Acceptance tests: we'll run your baseline queries and share a public eval dashboard; suggested pass/fail thresholds are configurable and we'll align on exact datasets in the deep-dive.

Technical notes for the deep-dive (so your engineers know what to expect):
- Embeddings -> Vector DB: we recommend provided embeddings with an ANN index (Faiss/HNSW). We'll show a toy pipeline and how reranking calls back into a smaller decoder model to improve precision on top-5.
- Streaming assistant: SDK supports streaming responses over HTTP/WS. For the UI, use incremental patch updates to the EHR middleware to avoid partial writes.
- Function calling/schema enforcement: see example schema in prototype-snippets; Redwood can validate the model output against a JSON schema and return a structured payload or an explicit error token to trigger a retry.

Suggested immediate action: confirm Thu 8/14 10am PT for the technical deep-dive — I booked the slot tentatively on our side.

Ben
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachment: redwood-nova-pilot-sow.pdf (draft pilot SOW and estimate)


From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 Aug 2025 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap and technical scope from today's Nova Health integration call

Ben / Laura —

Notes from my side ahead of the deep-dive (internal):

1) POC scope (6 weeks) proposed split:
- Week 1: onboarding, dataset exchange (secure upload), spin up a private test instance in our VPC, wire KMS keys.
- Weeks 2-3: embeddings index + reranker prototype; run baseline queries and log metrics.
- Week 4: structured extraction pipeline (function-calling) + integration to middleware sandbox.
- Weeks 5-6: tuning, acceptance testing, perf/latency optimization.

2) Technical risks / mitigations:
- Latency: to hit sub-300ms p95 we may need to quantize reranker and enable batching for decoder calls. Propose conservative config initially and a perf tuning sprint in week 5.
- Data residency: confirm whether Nova needs logs kept outside their VPC; if so we'll stub minimal logs and direct full audit logs to their S3.

3) Pre-deep-dive checklist (what I need from Nova):
- A sample of 200 de-identified clinical queries + ground-truth top-5 results.
- Middleware API spec (auth headers, payload contract).

I'll prepare a short demo notebook showing embeddings -> ANN -> reranker and a function-calling example that returns a validated JSON record.

Action items assigned:
- Dev: prep demo notebook and perf baseline by Thu morning.
- Laura: pull SOC2 snapshot + arch diagram and send to procurement.
- Ben: finalize SOW PDF and confirm deep-dive RSVP from María + procurement.

Thanks — see you on Thu if the slot holds.

- Dev
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
dev.patel@redwood.com

title: Retroactive Clawback & 3-strike Termination Request — OrthoCare
preview:
From: Asha Rao <asha.rao@orthocare.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: OrthoCare Procurement <procurement@orthocare.io>
Date: Sun, 3 Aug 2025 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: SLA incidents Jun–Jul: retroactive clawback and 3‑strike termination

Ben,

Thanks for taking this. Over the last two months we've logged multiple P1/P2 incidents that materially impacted OrthoCare's customer workflows (see attached incident-log_Jun-Jul_2025.csv).

Given the repeated degradation and the downstream regulatory exposure, procurement is asking for the following contract-grade remedies before proceeding with renewal:

- Retroactive clawback: liquidated damages covering the invoiced fees for the affected months (June + July) if root cause analysis doesn't show customer-impacting misconfiguration
- Immediate fee withholding right: ability to withhold invoices up to 20% pending remediation
- 3‑strike termination: if we observe 3 P1 incidents within any 90‑day rolling window, OrthoCare can terminate for cause and recover 3x monthly fees as liquidated damages

We'd like a written response and a proposed commercial/legal compromise by EOD 8/7. I'll loop in our counsel (Daniel) on any contract language.

-Asha

Attachments: incident-log_Jun-Jul_2025.csv

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Asha Rao <asha.rao@orthocare.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 Aug 2025 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SLA incidents Jun–Jul: retroactive clawback and 3‑strike termination

Asha — thanks for the detailed log and heads-up. We take repeat P1s seriously and want to get you a clear path forward.

Quick asks from our side before we draft language:
- Can you confirm which incidents you view as P1 (the spreadsheet has a few that our engineers classified as P2)
- Any regulatory citations you want called out in the contract language?

I'll loop Marissa (commercial/legal) and Vivek (engineer) to review. We'll aim to respond with a commercial counterproposal by EOD Friday (8/7).

Ben

Attachment referenced: SOW_v3.pdf (please attach if you have an updated SOW)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Asha Rao <asha.rao@orthocare.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 5 Aug 2025 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SLA incidents Jun–Jul: retroactive clawback and 3‑strike termination

Asha — thanks for the patience. After reviewing the incident log and consulting with engineering and legal, Redwood's position on the commercial asks is below:

- Retroactive liquidated damages: we cannot accept retroactive LDs tied to past months. Liquidated damages are forward‑looking and determined at signing; retroactive damages create open-ended exposure for us.

- Fee withholding: we can agree to a limited, time‑boxed holdback mechanism as an escrowed reserve up to 25% of monthly fees for a 90‑day period, with clear release conditions tied to RCA and remediation milestones. This is intended to protect OrthoCare without disrupting operations.

- 3‑strike termination: we can agree to a three‑incident termination right, but with a 30‑day cure period following the third incident and a requirement that incidents are severity‑confirmed by a joint incident review board. Termination would be for material, unresolved breaches only; we cannot agree to automatic 3x monthly fee LDs on termination.

I'll attach a redlined counterproposal (redwood-counterproposal-20250807.pdf) that codifies the escrow/holdback, cure timetable, and a capped termination refund (pro‑rated unused prepayments). If that does not address procurement's concerns, let's set a call with counsel.

-Marissa

Attachments: redwood-counterproposal-20250807.pdf

From: Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@orthocare.io>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Asha Rao <asha.rao@orthocare.io>
Date: Thu, 7 Aug 2025 08:44:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SLA incidents Jun–Jul: retroactive clawback and 3‑strike termination

Marissa — thanks for the quick turnaround. OrthoCare's legal position is firm on two points:

1) Retroactive remedy: given the regulatory risk tied to our end customers, we need a viable path to make OrthoCare whole for demonstrable customer‑impact that occurred in June/July. We're not asking for unlimited open exposure — a fixed liquidated damages sum (capped at 3x monthly fees) tied to demonstrable outages is reasonable.

2) Withholding right: procurement must be able to withhold invoiced amounts up to 20% pending final remediation. Escrowed solutions are workable if Redwood agrees to clear release criteria and a neutral escrow agent.

If Redwood is unwilling to accept a capped post‑hoc LD + withholding right, OrthoCare may have to escalate commercially. Please confirm whether Redwood will accept a capped LD and escrow agent by COB 8/10.

Regards,
Daniel

PS — we attached a short legal memo summarizing the regulatory exposure; happy to share on a call.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Asha Rao <asha.rao@orthocare.io>, Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@orthocare.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 11 Aug 2025 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SLA incidents Jun–Jul: retroactive clawback and 3‑strike termination — proposed compromise

Asha, Daniel — thanks for the candor. To move this to a resolution and keep your renewal on track, Redwood can offer the following compromise:

- No retroactive unlimited LDs. Instead, Redwood will accept a capped, demonstrable‑impact remedy for June/July equal to the lesser of actual billed fees for those months or 3x one monthly invoice; payment contingent on mutually agreed RCA showing customer‑level impact.

- Holdback/Escrow: Redwood will agree to an escrowed holdback of up to 15% of monthly fees for 90 days, administered by a neutral escrow agent (we propose EscrowTrust). Funds are released per a milestone schedule tied to remediation and RCA signoff.

- 3‑Strike termination: If OrthoCare experiences three confirmed P1s within any 60‑day window (we shortened window to balance risk), OrthoCare may terminate after a 30‑day cure period. Termination provides a pro‑rated refund of prepaid amounts and the capped remedy above; no automatic 3x LD payout.

- Operational commitment: Redwood will provide a prioritized SLA improvement plan with targeted fixes and commit a weekly status cadence during the 90‑day window.

If that is acceptable in principle, we'll produce redline language and updated SOW for signature. Please confirm by EOD 8/13 so we can get legal aligned.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments referenced: SOW_v3.pdf

preview:
From: Amira Hassan <amira.hassan@spectrahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-08-01T14:12:00Z
Subject: Inquiry: eligibility for service credit after Jul 29 outage

Hi Ben,

We noticed a sustained service degradation on Jul 29 that affected a batch of inference jobs (Redwood incident id Redwood-INC-7892). Our pipelines experienced ~80–90 minutes of elevated error/retry rates and delayed completions. Can you confirm whether SpectraHealth is eligible for an SLA/service credit and, if so, what the calculation would look like and the expected timeline to receive the credit?

Relevant details from our side: customer account SH-2024-09-118, invoice reference INV-20250731, primary contact cc'd. Happy to provide logs if helpful — we can share a sample trace.

Thanks,
Amira Hassan
Sr. SRE, SpectraHealth
amira.hassan@spectrahealth.com
+1 415-555-0112


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Amira Hassan <amira.hassan@spectrahealth.com>
Cc: ops@spectrahealth.com, procurement@spectrahealth.com
Date: 2025-08-01T20:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Inquiry: eligibility for service credit after Jul 29 outage

Hi Amira — thanks for the note and for flagging the incident id (Redwood-INC-7892). I'm sorry SpectraHealth hit that degradation window. Short answer: you are likely eligible for a service credit; I want to run the standard eligibility and calculation steps and then confirm a final amount.

What I'll do next:
- Pull the incident timeline and confirm the exact start/end used for entitlement (we'll use the customer-observed window and our trace to correlate).
- Run a credit calculation using the formula in our SLA (attached spreadsheet) and provide both the formulaic result and a proposed goodwill adjustment if the number looks small compared to operational impact.
- Loop in finance/ops for approval if the credit > $1k.

Quick notes on methodology (see attached 'spectra_sla_downtime_calc.xlsx'):
- Hosted (usage) customers: credit = (outage minutes / minutes in billing month) * monthly billed amount (or the prorated usage during affected window).
- Dedicated/committed customers: similar pro-rata approach against the committed monthly fee; for larger tiers we sometimes apply the SLA tier schedule (e.g., >X minutes = Y% credit) per contract.

To run the calc I need:
- Final confirmed outage window (start/end) from your side.
- Any additional downstream impact hours you want us to include beyond the core incident window.

I've attached a preliminary incident summary (incident-summary-Redwood-INC-7892.pdf) and a blank calculation sheet. If you can confirm the window and whether to include the 30-minute tail of retries, I'll produce the numbers by EOD tomorrow.

Regards,
Ben Carter
Account Lead, Commercial Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
Mobile: +1 415-555-0199

Attachment: spectra_sla_downtime_calc.xlsx (Excel)
Attachment: incident-summary-Redwood-INC-7892.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-08-02T09:30:00Z
Subject: Fwd: SpectraHealth SLA credit calc — preliminary numbers and approval request

Marissa / Vivek — looping you in. SpectraHealth (SH-2024-09-118) hit Redwood-INC-7892 on Jul 29; Amira asked about eligibility. I attached the incident summary and ran the quick calc against July billing minutes. Numbers below are preliminary and conservative.

Summary (prelim):
- Confirmed outage window used: 2025-07-29T03:45Z — 2025-07-29T05:08Z = 83 minutes total (we agreed to include the 30-minute retry tail in the window).
- Minutes in July (31 days): 44,640
- Monthly committed fee (SpectraHealth Dedicated tier): $12,000/month (from contract)
- Formulaic pro-rata credit = (83 / 44,640) * $12,000 = $22.34

Context: formulaic credit here is tiny relative to the operational disruption. In prior cases we've offered a one-time operational goodwill credit to preserve the relationship — typically between $1k–$5k depending on impact. Given SpectraHealth's growth and the visibility of this outage, I recommend approving a one-time $1,500 credit and applying the formulaic pro-rata amount as the documented SLA credit (we'll issue the larger goodwill amount as a separate credit memo to keep contract math clean).

Request: can you confirm approval to issue a $1,500 one-time goodwill credit plus the formal prorated SLA credit (combined on a single credit memo) and route the approval to billing? If you prefer, I can reduce the goodwill amount. Since this > $1k, I need sign-off from finance/ops.

I've attached the calc workbook (filled) for your review. If approved I will notify Amira with timeline: credit memo issued within 3 business days, invoice adjustment applied on next cycle.

Thanks,
Ben

- Dedicated/committed customers: similar pro-rata approach against the committed monthly fee; for larger tiers we sometimes apply the SLA tier schedule (e.g., >X minutes = Y% credit) per contract.

To run the calc I need:
- Final confirmed outage window (start/end) from your side.
- Any additional downstream impact hours you want us to include beyond the core incident window.

I've attached a preliminary incident summary (incident-summary-Redwood-INC-7892.pdf) and a blank calculation sheet. If you can confirm the window and whether to include the 30-minute tail of retries, I'll produce the numbers by EOD tomorrow.
