title: SOC2 applicability for deployment modes (Hosted / Dedicated / Private)
preview:
From: Elena Torres <security@acme-corp.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jul 2025 12:18:00 -0700
Subject: SOC2 report + scope question for Dedicated/Private deployments

Hi Alex / Redwood team,

We're in the final stages of our vendor security review for Acme's product expansion. Two quick asks so procurement can close:

1) Can you confirm whether Redwood's SOC 2 Type II report covers Dedicated (reserved GPU pools) that we've contracted with you? Our security team assumes the SOC 2 report covers only multi-tenant hosted services unless otherwise stated.
2) For Private (VPC/on-prem) deployments, do you provide a bridge letter or any attestation we can present to auditors?

If helpful, please point us to the specific section/pages in your SOC2 where scope boundaries are described. We need answers by EOD Aug 3 for the procurement deadline.

Thanks,
Elena Torres
Head of Security Programs
Acme Corp

--
Elena Torres
Acme Corp | security@acme-corp.com


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Torres <security@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jul 2025 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SOC2 report + scope question for Dedicated/Private deployments

Hi Elena — thanks for the note and timeline. Attaching our latest redacted SOC 2 Type II report and a draft bridge-letter template for Private deployments (see attachments). A few quick points while Laura and Marcus confirm the nitty-gritty: 

- Hosted (Redwood-managed multi-tenant API): covered by the SOC 2 Type II report for the audit period (report attached).
- Dedicated ("Redwood Dedicated" reservations): typically covered when the customer purchases dedicated capacity that is provisioned and operated by Redwood within our control plane (same control environment as Hosted). If the customer provisions Dedicated but manages the underlying infrastructure (customer-managed on their cloud account), that configuration is out-of-scope for Redwood's SOC 2.
- Private / VPC / on-prem: out-of-scope for Redwood's SOC 2. We can provide a bridge letter and evidence bundle to support your audit.

Laura / Marcus — can you confirm the exact language we should send to customers and whether we can provide a one-page scope statement to include in procurement? Elena — I'll follow up with a formal note and SLA/contract implications by Aug 3.

Best,
Alex
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0147

Attachments: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2024-07-redacted.pdf, SOC2-bridge-letter-template.docx


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 31 Jul 2025 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: recommended scope language and evidence for Acme

Alex / Marcus — drafting recommended customer-facing language and evidence checklist below. Please use this as the reply to Acme; I also uploaded the detailed mapping workbook to our shared Drive (see link).

Recommended plain-language summary for customers:
- Redwood's SOC 2 Type II (report attached) covers Redwood-managed inference services and the control environment operated by Redwood during the audit period (controls around change management, logical access, encryption, incident response, etc.).
- For Redwood Dedicated where capacity is reserved in Redwood's tenancy and Redwood operates the GPU pools, those instances are included within the scope of the SOC 2 report.
- Customer-managed Dedicated (where the customer runs Redwood software in their own cloud account or on-prem infrastructure) and Private / VPC / on-prem deployments are NOT covered by Redwood's SOC 2. For those deployment modes we provide:
  * A bridge letter describing which Redwood controls remain applicable and which are the customer's responsibility.
  * A configuration checklist and evidence bundle showing how Redwood secures the control plane components we still operate.
  * Assistance during customer audits (evidence collection, control descriptions) under an NDA.

Evidence checklist to offer Acme (we can provide redacted items where needed):
- Redacted SOC2 Type II report (attached)
- Management's description of controls (one-pager scope statement)
- Bridge letter (template attached)
- Control mapping workbook (scope-mapping-dedicated-hosted.xlsx)
- Sample logs/traces for control-plane events (time-limited access)

Timelines / process notes:
- Bridge letter: 3–5 business days for a templated version; fully customized bridge letter (legal review) may take 2–3 weeks.
- Evidence bundle: usually 5–10 business days depending on redaction requests.

Marcus — please confirm the canonical wording for the contract clause that indicates when Dedicated is Redwood-managed vs customer-managed, so we don't create procurement back-and-forth.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Director, Security & Compliance | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Torres <security@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 3 Aug 2025 15:04:00 -0700
Subject: Final: SOC2 scope boundaries and next steps (Hosted / Dedicated / Private)

Elena — quick follow-up with the clarified, customer-ready summary and next steps (per Laura's recommendations). I've also attached the redacted SOC 2 report and the bridge-letter template.

Customer-ready scope summary (please feel free to copy into your procurement docs):
- Redwood SOC 2 Type II covers Redwood-managed Hosted inference and any Dedicated capacity that is provisioned and operated by Redwood in Redwood's tenancy.
- Deployments where the customer provisions or manages the infrastructure (customer-managed Dedicated, VPC-connected self-hosted instances, or fully on-prem installations) are not covered by Redwood's SOC 2. These deployments require a bridge letter or customer attestation for audit purposes.

Offered deliverables and timeline for Acme: 
- Deliverable: templated bridge letter + one-page scope statement. ETA: 3 business days.
- Deliverable: redacted evidence bundle (SOC2 report, control descriptions, mapping workbook). ETA: 7 business days after confirmation of redaction scope.
- Request from Acme: please confirm which Dedicated instance(s) you have with Redwood and whether they are operated by Redwood (we need tenancy/account IDs). That will determine whether we can include them in the SOC 2 evidence package.

Next steps:
1) Reply with the Dedicated instance identifiers (or confirm none are customer-managed).
2) Tell us any special redaction requirements for PII or commercial terms.
3) We'll issue the templated bridge letter and scope one-pager once we have the Dedicated IDs.

If useful, happy to set a quick 20-minute call with Laura and Marcus to walk your audit team through the mapping.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0147

Attachments: Redacted SOC2 (Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2024-07-redacted.pdf), SOC2-bridge-letter-template.docx, scope-mapping-dedicated-hosted.xlsx

title: Pilot extension: milestone-linked extension counterproposal
preview:
From: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@clearpath.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@clearpath.ai
Date: 2025-07-30T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Pilot runway / extension ask — ClearPath

Ben,

Appreciate the work so far — the team hit the integration milestone but we still need a bit more live traffic to validate latency under real load. Procurement asked me to request one of these:

- Option A: 30 extra days of pilot time + 40% discount on incremental usage during the extension; or
- Option B: 2-week extension + $30k credit bucket applied to token usage (credits must be escrowed).

We prefer Option B but procurement is worried about credit accounting unless there's a clear clause tying credits to pilot metrics. Can you propose firm language or an alternative that helps us avoid a large invoice if we don't convert?

Attaching our usage dump and a short note from infra. Looking for a quick reply today if possible — execs want to know cost exposure before signing the NDA addendum.

Thanks,
Alyssa
Alyssa Chen
VP, Platform Engineering
ClearPath AI

Attachment: ClearPath_usage_report_Jul2025.csv


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@clearpath.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>, procurement@clearpath.ai
Date: 2025-07-30T12:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot runway / extension ask — ClearPath

Alyssa — thanks for the context and the usage file. Congrats on getting to the integration milestone. Quick thoughts: we can do both flavors but suggest a milestone-tied approach that lowers your procurement risk while keeping runway for engineering to validate. Two clear counterproposals below.

Counterproposal 1 (preferred, lower vendor risk): milestone-linked incremental credits
- 14-day extension unlocked immediately for engineering validation
- $30k credit placed in a short-term escrow and released to Redwood only if the pilot hits two success gates within the extension window: (1) 99th percentile latency under 750ms for two consecutive business days, and (2) conversion of one internal integration test into a production flow (dev-signed checklist)
- If gates are not met, credits are voided and no charge occurs
- No upfront invoice — DocuSign addendum with escrow instructions

Counterproposal 2 (longer runway, higher discount): timebox + conditional discount
- 30-day extension starts on sign
- 40% discount on incremental usage during the 30 days, capped at $45k total exposure
- If conversion occurs within the 30 days, discount converts to a one-time credit applied to first month post-contract

Ask: which path reduces procurement friction for you? If escrow is a blocker we can convert the escrow clause into an automated refund schedule (Redwood invoices, then we issue a credit if gates are unmet) — that adds a paper trail for procurement. I can circulate a short DocuSign addendum for either option.

Jonas, looping you for SLA/finops comfort on the escrow language and whether Legal will need anything extra.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Redwood_pilot_addendum_v1.docx (draft addendum template)


From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@clearpath.ai>, procurement@clearpath.ai
Date: 2025-07-30T14:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot runway / extension ask — ClearPath

Ben / Alyssa — I'm comfortable with Counterproposal 1 as the cleanest way to limit procurement exposure while giving the team runway. A couple of operational clarifications we should add to the addendum:

- Definition of success gates (precise metrics + measurement window) so there's no ambiguity; include the exact telemetry dashboard link and which Redwood metric (eg: model_inference.99p_latency) is authoritative.
- Escrow mechanics: procurement can use a third-party escrow (we're fine) OR we can hold the credit on our ledger and only invoice if gate conditions are met. If procurement prefers ledger-credit, we'll provide monthly usage reports and a one-click refund if gates aren't satisfied.
- If the gates are narrowly missed by <5% on a single day, allow a 48-hour remediation window before voiding credits (reasonable engineering wiggle room).

I can have Legal draft the short clause; Ben — if you can confirm the exact gateways they want (Alyssa?), I'll turn it into redlines and a DocuSign-ready addendum.

—Jonas
Jonas Weber
Director, Sales Strategy
Redwood Inference


From: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@clearpath.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>, procurement@clearpath.ai
Date: 2025-07-31T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot runway / extension ask — ClearPath

Thanks Ben/Jonas — procurement reviewed both options and prefers the milestone-linked incremental credits (Counterproposal 1) but with the 48-hour remediation window Jonas mentioned. The team confirms the two gates as: (1) 99p latency <= 750ms measured over a rolling 24h window; (2) a signed internal runbook + one successful production-like end-to-end job (defined in attached checklist).

Next steps for us: please send the DocuSign addendum with the escrow/ledger option spelled out and a note that the credits will be voided if gates are not met after the remediation window. We can sign quickly if procurement sees the ledger option and the remediation clause.

I'll loop Legal after signature to confirm the technical checklist.

Best,
Alyssa
Alyssa Chen
VP, Platform Engineering
ClearPath AI

Hi Marta / Tom —

Thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our discovery call and a proposed path forward so we can lock the pilot schedule:

Recap (what we heard)
- Primary goal: reduce triage time in their clinical intake workflows by surfacing suggested summaries and flagging high-priority cases.
- Success metric: 30% reduction in manual triage time for first-pass reviewers (measured by average minutes per case).
- Scope: 2 teams, ~150 daily cases, sample PHI redaction required during test.
- Key stakeholders: Marta (clinical lead), Tom (IT / data custodian), plus legal for SAs.

2) Mutual action plan spreadsheet (attached) — fills in owners and dates; I’ve proposed a 6-week pilot starting Aug 11.

I’ll wait for your confirmations and will circulate an updated MAP and calendar invites once we lock dates.

Ben

title: Security exhibit / pen-test window for MSA & Order Form
preview:
From: Ava Morgan <ava.morgan@medisync.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: security exhibit + pen test window for MSA/order form

Hi Ben / Laura,

Thanks for moving fast on the MSA. Security has a few required clarifications before we can sign:

- Audit / inspection rights: we need language that allows a quarterly SOC 2 review plus an annual onsite inspection or a remote evidence sprint. Can you confirm what you can provide and the typical evidence cadence?
- Pen test language: customer-run pen tests are covered in our template — we need a 30-day notice window and a short window where Redwood will provide staging access. Can Redwood accept this or propose an equivalent?
- Sampling and scope: we need to understand boundaries (prod vs staging), and whether Redwood can carve out any infra that cannot be inspected for security reasons.
- Encryption / keys: requirement to preserve customer-owned keys for regulated data — is there any restriction we should be aware of?

I've attached our audit requirements spreadsheet (Medisync_Audit_Reqs.xlsx) and a short SOC2 request form. Would appreciate a consolidated reply so we can loop Legal and Security on our side.

Best,
Ava Morgan
Head of Security Programs
Medisync Health

--
Attachment: Medisync_Audit_Reqs.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)
Attachment: SOC2_Request_Form.pdf (form stub)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Morgan <ava.morgan@medisync.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jul 2025 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: security exhibit + pen test window for MSA/order form

Ava — thanks for the clear ask. Quick proposal from our side so we can converge:

1) Evidence cadence: Redwood can commit to quarterly evidence bundles (remote) and an annual evidence sprint that can be remote or in-person with reasonable notice. Typical remote evidence sprint is 10 business days for delivery of documents and role-based access for verification.
2) Pen-test: we can accept customer-run external tests against a customer-dedicated staging environment. We ask for 30 days notice + a 72-hour blackout window after exploit disclosure for remediation planning. Tests against shared production are not permitted.
3) Sampling & scope: carve out any infra components that we classify as provider-managed control plane; we will clearly call out non-inspectable components in the order form appendix.
4) Keys: support BYOK for customer-owned keys in transit and at rest; we will confirm exact KMS integrations in the order form.

Next steps I propose:
- Laura: legal redline for the MSA/Order Form security exhibit and suggested language for inspection/notice/blackout.
- Grace: quick run of the pen-test runbook and what we can provide for staging access.
- I will share a proposed order form excerpt (attached) with recommended text and an escalation ladder for anything that needs exec attention.

Call availability: I'm free today 3–4pm PT or tomorrow morning — happy to jump on a short call with Ava and legal.

Thanks,
Ben
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: proposed_orderform_excerpt.pdf (excerpt stub)


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Ava Morgan <ava.morgan@medisync.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jul 2025 14:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: security exhibit + pen test window for MSA/order form

Ava / Ben — looping in legal comments and a redline summary first pass. High level: we want to be cooperative but need to limit open-ended inspection rights and preserve operational safety. Suggested edits below (summary only; full mark-up attached).

- Inspection cadence: replace open quarterly/annual "inspection rights" wording with a structured evidence cadence: quarterly remote evidence bundles + one scheduled evidence sprint per 12 months (remote by default). We will accept an optional onsite review for exceptional regulatory needs but only with 90 days' advance notice and mutually agreed scope.
- Pen-test notice: propose 30 days' notice for customer-run external tests against a customer-dedicated staging environment; Redwood requires the right to request a 7-day postponement once for safety/operational reasons.
- Scope carveouts: add explicit language that excludes Redwood-managed control plane services and any third-party infrastructure Redwood does not control. Those exclusions will be listed in a secure appendix to the order form.
- Data & keys: accept BYOK for customer-controlled encryption; add additional language that customer-managed keys cannot be used to break Redwood platform guarantees (e.g., we require ability to access keys for platform maintenance only under emergency procedures -- propose defining those emergency procedures in a separate runbook).
- Remediation / blackout: require a 72-hour coordinated disclosure window to allow Redwood to triage exploitable findings before external disclosure.

I attached a tracked-change document with the proposed MSA redline (redlines_MSA_changes.docx). Questions for Ava: do you have a list of non-negotiable audit clauses from your procurement/legal team? If so please flag them so we can surface at the exec level.

Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

> On Tue, Jul 29, 2025 at 10:03 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Ava — thanks for the clear ask. Quick proposal from our side so we can converge:
> > ...

--
Attachment: redlines_MSA_changes.docx (docx tracked changes stub)


From: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Morgan <ava.morgan@medisync.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jul 2025 15:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: security exhibit + pen test window for MSA/order form

Thanks — a few operational points from security/infra that I want captured before we finalize language:

- Staging environment: we can provide a customer-dedicated staging stack for pen tests. That environment is ephemeral; we need at least 7 business days lead time to provision. Staging will include representative data sets but sanitized PII unless a specific DPA addendum permits otherwise.
- Allowed test types: we are OK with external blackbox and whitebox tests against staging, but will prohibit social engineering, phishing simulations, and DDoS as they risk platform customers. We'll enumerate acceptable test classes in the exhibit.
- Evidence cadence: security recommends a 10-business-day remote evidence sprint deliverable for the annual review, with a pre-canned evidence bundle for quarterly checks (SOC2 report + control narratives + sample logs).
- Escalation ladder (proposed):
  1) Primary: Ben Carter (ben_carter@redwood.com) — initial coordination and schedule
  2) Security ops: Grace O'Connor (grace_oconnor@redwood.com) — technical point for access/config
  3) Legal: Laura Bennett (laura_bennett@redwood.com) — contract/legal clarifications
  4) Exec escalation: VP of Sales (vp_sales@redwood.com) / Chief Security Officer (cso@redwood.com) — for unresolved exceptions

- Exceptions process: propose a formal exception request template (short form) — operations will review within 5 business days and escalate to legal if required.

I'll attach the evidence runbook and a short staging checklist. Ben, if you want I can draft a single-page exhibit appendix that matches Laura's redline and these operational constraints.

Grace
Head of Security Operations, Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: evidence-runbook.md (markdown stub)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Morgan <ava.morgan@medisync.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jul 2025 15:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: security exhibit + pen test window for MSA/order form

Perfect — thanks everyone. I'll: 
- Send the proposed exhibit appendix (incorporating Laura's redline and Grace's operational notes) to Ava by EOD
- Propose a 30-minute call tomorrow to walk through the staging timeline and sign-off process

Ava — would 10am PT tomorrow work for a quick sync?

Appreciate the collaboration.

Ben
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

title: MAP handshake: Evaluation & close lane for HealthBridge — criteria and tests
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@healthbridge.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP handshake: Evaluation & close lane for HealthBridge — criteria and tests

Hi Sara,

Great to meet yesterday — thanks for the time. Attached is a first-pass Mutual Action Plan (MAP) and a proposed evaluation test suite to make the pilot and close predictable. High level items I want us aligned on:

- Timeline (proposed): kickoff week of Aug 4 -> 3-week evaluation window -> decision week of Aug 25
- Success criteria (exit gates):
  - Functional coverage: target >= 90% on the agreed intent set
  - Quality: agreement (intent-match) >= 95% on the evaluation set
  - Safety: hallucination rate < 2% measured against our sample queries
  - Performance: 99th-pct latency <= 300ms for typical 256-token requests
  - Reliability: error rate < 0.5% during the evaluation window
- Acceptance tests (examples): integration smoke, canary traffic run (10% -> 50%), end-to-end user flow tests, labeled eval concordance checks, throughput ramp tests
- Security/compliance needs: SOC2 attestation and simple dataflow diagram for procurement

Attachments: MAP_Draft_HealthBridge_v1.pdf (MAP), evaluation_test_suite.xlsx (test owner matrix & initial scripts).

Could you confirm:
1) Procurement timeline on your side (how long to get an order / PO signed)?
2) Any additional success criteria you need for internal sign-off (e.g., legal/risk must see specific artifacts)?

If procurement can commit by Aug 15, we can target an Aug 25 cutover with a staged roll. I cc'ed Grace to scope the canary/ops plan and Marissa for acceptance tests.

Thanks — looking forward to locking the lane.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments: MAP_Draft_HealthBridge_v1.pdf (pdf), evaluation_test_suite.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Sara Patel <sara@healthbridge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Tim Lee <tim.lee@healthbridge.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Jul 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP handshake: Evaluation & close lane for HealthBridge — criteria and tests

Ben — thanks, this looks clear. A few quick notes from our side:

- Procurement/Legal timeline: ~3–5 weeks to route an SOW + PO. I expect we could have a signed PO by mid-August if nothing unexpected shows up. I looped Tim (procurement) above.
- Pilot start: Aug 4 works for our product team.
- Additional sign-off asks from Risk: an architecture/dataflow diagram and SOC2/attestation statement are required before any production-like traffic.
- Success gates: we agree on most of the targets but would like to add a rollback condition — if hallucination rate > 3% on the closed eval set OR if false-positive critical errors occur > 0.2% over a 24-hour window, we pause and remediate.

I attached a short procurement intake form (procurement_request.docx) that Tim usually uses. If you can attach the dataflow diagram and SOC2 snapshot, that will speed legal review.

Best,
Sara
Product Owner | HealthBridge

Subject: Re: MAP handshake: Evaluation & close lane for HealthBridge — criteria and tests

Adding the acceptance checklist (summary below). Full spreadsheet attached: acceptance_tests_healthbridge.xlsx.

Acceptance checklist (owners & pass thresholds):
- Labeled concordance (Marissa) — pass if intent-match >= 95% across evaluation corpus (sample size 1,200)
- Safety/hallucination (Marissa) — pass if hallucination rate < 2% on labeled safety queries; rollback if > 3% per Sara's ask
- Integration smoke (Grace) — pass if 10 critical flows succeed end-to-end in staging for 48h
- Performance (Monica) — pass if 99p latency <= 350ms at baseline traffic and throughput >= 50 r/s sustained for 30m during the ramp
- Error rate (Grace/Monica) — pass if 24h error rate < 0.5% and no critical severity incidents
- Security (Ben) — pass when SOC2 snapshot + dataflow diagram are acknowledged by HealthBridge legal

Operational runbook notes:
- Canary window: 2 x 24h windows at 10% traffic, automated alerts for any degradation; manual stop if rollback triggers hit.
- Measurement: daily concordance report and a 72h burn-in metric pack for final signoff.

I'll own the labeled evaluation, will publish interim reports every 48 hours to the shared drive.

—Marissa

3) Canary: 10% traffic for 48h -> monitor errors/latency (auto rollback conditions)
4) Ramp to 50% / full cut after pass and PO signed

—Ben

Adding the acceptance checklist (summary below). Full spreadsheet attached: acceptance_tests_healthbridge.xlsx.

Acceptance checklist (owners & pass thresholds):
- Labeled concordance (Marissa) — pass if intent-match >= 95% across evaluation corpus (sample size 1,200)
- Safety/hallucination (Marissa) — pass if hallucination rate < 2% on labeled safety queries; rollback if > 3% per Sara's ask
- Integration smoke (Grace) — pass if 10 critical flows succeed end-to-end in staging for 48h
- Performance (Monica) — pass if 99p latency <= 350ms at baseline traffic and throughput >= 50 r/s sustained for 30m during the ramp
- Error rate (Grace/Monica) — pass if 24h error rate < 0.5% and no critical severity incidents
- Security (Ben) — pass when SOC2 snapshot + dataflow diagram are acknowledged by HealthBridge legal

Operational runbook notes:
- Canary window: 2 x 24h windows at 10% traffic, automated alerts for any degradation; manual stop if rollback triggers hit.
- Measurement: daily concordance report and a 72h burn-in metric pack for final signoff.

title: MSA + Order Form: proposed 'press blackout' clause and logo exemplar language
preview:
From: Anna Torres <anna@brightworks.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@brightworks.com>, BrightWorks Legal <legal@brightworks.com>
Date: 2025-07-19T08:11:00-07:00
Subject: RE: MSA + Order Form — publicity / logo language

Hi Chloe,

Thanks for sending the draft MSA and Order Form. Our legal team reviewed and flagged two items that are blocking internal marketing approval: the publicity clause and the logo usage grant. Specifically: 

- Publicity: the current clause grants \"perpetual, worldwide\" rights to use our name and logo in any marketing materials. We can agree to customer references and co-marketing, but need a narrow scope: 24 months post-termination, and explicit approval of any press release quoting BrightWorks.

- Logo use: we can provide a high-res logo for case studies and partner pages but require brand guidelines to be followed and any produced asset to be sent to our brand team for review before publishing. We also need an explicit embargo period for press releases tied to joint announcements (we prefer 30–60 days after launch, depending on the program).

Attached is our redline (BrightWorks_MSA_redline_20250719.docx) and our brand spec (BW-Logo-Guidelines-v2.pdf). Can you confirm whether Redwood can accept a 24-month reference window and a required approval step for press releases (BrightWorks to respond within 5 business days), plus an explicit embargo option for mutually agreed announcements?

If helpful, Tom and I are available for a 30-minute call tomorrow to walk legal through the wording.

Best,
Anna Torres
Director, Partnerships
BrightWorks

Attachments: BrightWorks_MSA_redline_20250719.docx (redline), BW-Logo-Guidelines-v2.pdf (branding guidelines)

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
To: Anna Torres <anna@brightworks.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-07-19T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form: publicity / logo language — proposed approach

Hi Anna — thanks for the redlines and the brand guide. Appreciate the clarity. Looping in Ben (sales/legal lead on order forms) and Marissa (contracts) to confirm our position.

Quick summary of what we can do without an exec escalation: 

1) Reference window: we can change \"perpetual\" to a time-bound window. Our standard is 36 months, but I can push to 24 months for BrightWorks as part of this deal.

2) Press releases: we are comfortable with an approval step and SLA — BrightWorks to approve within 5 business days, otherwise approval is deemed granted. For embargoes, we propose a mutually agreed date in the Order Form and the right for either party to request a reasonable extension with notice; typical default embargo we accept is 60 days after public beta/launch.

3) Logo use: we will include the brand guidelines language and add a requirement that any use of logos in marketing collateral must be sent to BrightWorks for a single review round before publication. We cannot accept a clause that allows BrightWorks to withhold approval indefinitely; we will add objective criteria and the 5-business-day SLA.

Action items: 
- Ben/Marissa: please suggest redline language for the publicity and logo clauses that implements the 24-month window, 5-business-day approval SLA, and 60-day default embargo with mutual extension. 
- Anna / BrightWorks Legal: if 24 months/5-business-day SLA/60-day embargo is acceptable, confirm so we can finalize and send to your legal for a fast sign-off.

I attached our suggested starting template (see related link to Google Doc). If all good, I can schedule a 30-min sync Friday to finalize.

Thanks —
Chloe
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachment referenced: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ExampleDocID (draft publicity & logo language)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.marissa@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-07-19T11:25:00-07:00
Subject: RE: MSA + Order Form — redline language suggestions

Chloe / team — drafting redline below for the publicity and logo sections. Pasting language to keep the thread self-contained. Please review and flag any business tradeoffs.

--- PROPOSED PUBLICITY/REFERENCE CLAUSE ---

\"Subject to the terms of this Agreement, Customer grants Redwood a non-exclusive, worldwide right to use Customer's name and logo for Redwood's marketing, sales and promotional materials solely for reference purposes for a period of twenty-four (24) months following the termination or expiration of the applicable Order Form. Prior to any public announcement or press release referencing Customer, Redwood shall provide Customer with the final form of such announcement for review. Customer shall provide any approval or requested changes within five (5) business days following receipt; failure to respond within such five (5) business day period shall be deemed approval. Notwithstanding the foregoing, both parties may agree in the applicable Order Form to a mutually agreed embargo period for specified joint announcements.\"

--- PROPOSED LOGO USAGE CLAUSE ---

\"Customer shall provide Redwood with Customer's logo files and brand guidelines. Redwood shall use Customer's logo only in accordance with such guidelines. Prior to publication of any marketing collateral that includes Customer's logo, Redwood shall submit such collateral to Customer for a single review round. Customer shall provide any comments within five (5) business days; failure to respond shall be deemed approval. In the event Customer requests changes, Redwood shall make commercially reasonable revisions and resubmit for approval. Any disallowed use (e.g., modifications that alter the logo as defined in Customer's guidelines) is prohibited without Customer's prior written consent.\"

--- END ---

Notes: 
- I kept the deemed-approval mechanism to protect our go-to-market timelines. 
- I limited to one review round to avoid endless back-and-forth. 
- If they insist on perpetual usage for a customer reference directory (not press), we can offer perpetual listing on a partner page only, with a separate opt-in box on the Order Form.

Marissa / Grace: please confirm the export of this proposed language into the MSA redline doc and that it won't create conflicts with our existing publicity framework.

Ben
VP, Sales Operations



From: BrightWorks Legal <legal@brightworks.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>, Anna Torres <anna@brightworks.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@brightworks.com>
Date: 2025-07-20T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: RE: MSA + Order Form — publicity / logo language (counter)

Chloe / team,

Thanks for the proposed language. Our counsel reviewed and provided a small counter on two points: 

1) Deemed approval: we understand the need for timelines, but a blanket \"deemed approval\" could be risky for us if a press release contains specific customer performance metrics. We propose an exception: deemed approval applies except where the release contains performance claims about BrightWorks' results; in that case BrightWorks must explicitly approve.

2) Embargo duration: 60 days is acceptable for product launches, but for co-marketed case studies around procurement cycles, we request flexibility for a 30-day embargo as a default in the OF, with the ability to extend to 60 days when a joint campaign is agreed. 

If those two changes are acceptable, we can sign off quickly. We also attached a clean version of the small edits (same filename: BrightWorks_MSA_redline_20250719.docx).

Anna / Tom — happy to hop on the call Friday at 10am PT if that helps finalize.

Regards,
BrightWorks Legal (on behalf of Anna Torres)

Quoted text below.

> 
> Thanks for sending the draft MSA and Order Form. Our legal team reviewed and flagged two items...

Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form: publicity / logo language — next steps

Thanks everyone — good progress. Quick recap and the path to signature: 

- We accept BrightWorks' carveout for performance claims: deemed approval will not apply to releases that include specific performance or benchmark claims; those require explicit sign-off.
- For embargo, we'll set the default in the Order Form to 30 days and include a checkbox to opt into a 60-day embargo for campaigns where agreed (this gives sales a configurable option).
- We'll import Ben's proposed wording into the MSA redline and incorporate the two exceptions above. Marissa will push the redline to the shared Doc and to your counsel.

Action items:

title: Scheduling: technical deep dive and rotating demo windows
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Moreno <alex@novamerge.com>, Priyanka Rao <priya@novamerge.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Thanks for today — proposed next steps + scheduling

Hi Alex / Priyanka,

Thanks for the call earlier — good overview of NovaMerge's realtime summarization pipeline and the latency/cost pain points you described. Quick recap:
- Primary goal: reduce tail latency for live transcripts while cutting per-token cost for archived re-runs.
- Constraints: HIPAA-adjacent workloads, US + EU data residency, ramp to ~100 concurrent inference sessions in peak.

Next steps I suggest:
1) Technical deep dive (60–90m) with engineering + infra (we'll bring a runtime engineer)
2) Pilot plan outline (scope, success metrics, timeline)

For scheduling the deep dive — to make this practical for your distributed team, we typically propose a rotating demo cadence so the same Redwood folks can attend across zones without burning anyone nightly. Proposed pattern (we can adjust):
- Week A: US-friendly window (Wed 10:00–11:30 PT)
- Week B: EU-friendly window (Thu 15:00–16:30 BST)
- Week C: APAC-friendly window (Fri 18:00–19:30 IST / SGT)

Can you share a couple of preferred 60–90m slots next week that fit your engineers? If easier I can propose three concrete options and send calendar invites with rotating recurrence.

I'm attaching a short deck and a quick .ics with the three sample windows.

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Cc: Priyanka Rao <priya@novamerge.com>

Subject: Re: Thanks for today — proposed next steps + scheduling

Thanks Ben — great to talk.

We're keen to dig in on the technical side. A few quick notes on availability:
- Engineering (myself + Priyanka) are best Mon/Tue mornings US PT or Wed afternoons UK time.
- We do have an on-call rotation; prefer to avoid late-evening APAC slots for engineers.

Concrete availability next week (60–90m):
- Tue 22 Jul: 09:30–11:00 PT (works for Priyanka)
- Wed 23 Jul: 15:00–16:30 BST (works for our EU team)

If you can lock one of those, we'll make sure the infra folks are available. Also, we have two compliance questions specific to pilot data handling — would be good if your CS/security person could be on the call for 15 min.

Cheers,
Alex

title: SignalSuite vendor replacement: pilot ask & packaging options
preview:
From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Hi Camila,

Quick note — we are evaluating a potential switch off ModelWave for our inference needs. We run a mix of long chat sessions (avg 700 tokens) and embedding-heavy batch jobs; current spend is roughly $110k/mo and ModelWave is offering a multi-year sticker that isn’t aligning with our burst/surge profile.

Two asks up front:
- Can you share packaging options that map to sustained commit vs bursty traffic?
- Would Redwood consider a constrained pilot (3 months) with migration credits to offset egress and implementation risk?

Attached is an excerpt of our current contract (ModelWave_contract_excerpt.pdf) — procurement wants a comparable commercial structure before we proceed.

Available for a quick call Wed afternoon.

Thanks,
Alex Turner
Head of ML Platforms
SignalSuite


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2025 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Alex — thanks for the note and the contract excerpt. Appreciate the usage breakdown in the file; that helps. A few quick points below and I can put together a short commercial two-pager.

High level packaging that typically maps to your profile:

1) Tiered Commit + Burst Pool
   - Monthly committed tokens with a small committed throughput SLA.
   - Shared burst pool for spikes billed at a higher token rate; can be capped per month.

2) Pilot (time-boxed) + Credits
   - 3-month pilot at a discount tier, with migration credits applied against first invoice or onboarding services.

3) Dedicated Reserved (if you want predictable latency)
   - Reserved GPU capacity with flat run-rate; suitable if you value tail-latency guarantees over unit cost.

Attached: redwood_pricing_snapshot_v1.xlsx — this is a starter view (estimates only).

If procurement wants to see an explicit pilot structure I can propose: 50% token-rate discount for 3 months + $25k migration credit, conditioned on a 12-month commit post-pilot. Does that sound like a starting point to you?

I can jump on Wed 2pm PT.

—Camila
Camila Reyes
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2025 08:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Looping in to offer a concession framework and quick trade-off guidance.

Camila — recommend we put two structured options in the deck so procurement can sign off:
A) Pilot-first route (lowest friction): 3-month pilot, 50% off token rates, $25k migration credit, auto-convert to 12-month 40% commit discount if usage >= 80% of pilot avg.
B) Commit-first route (best unit economics): 12-month commit at tiered pricing with a 6-month ramp and a smaller one-time onboarding credit ($10k).

If SignalSuite is worried about egress/onboarding risk we can add a short SLA credit (service credit up to 5% of monthly invoice) for first 90 days post-conversion.

Camila — happy to draft a short concession table. Alex — is procurement comfortable with a conditional auto-convert?

Jordan Blake
Head of Sales, Enterprise | Redwood Inference


From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2025 09:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Thanks Camila/Jordan — that framework is helpful. Procurement reviewed and had two clarifications:

1) They want the pilot to include at least 250k embedding units per month and clear overage pricing.
2) Procurement is pushing for migration credits to cover egress and initial consulting (they suggested $40k total).

Timeline: procurement needs a signed pilot addendum by the end of the month to stay on track for a Q4 transition.

I attached a short procurement note (procurement_terms.docx) outlining our internal approvals. Can you confirm whether the auto-convert clause requires additional legal approval on Redwood’s side?

—Alex


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2025 17:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Alex — appreciate the clarity. Quick answers and next steps below.

- Pilot scope: we can commit to 250k embedding units/month and the chat workload described. Overage rates will be our standard burst rate (1.8x pilot token rate) and we’ll cap monthly overage at 30% of committed pilot consumption unless you opt out.

- Migration credit: we can increase the onboarding credit to $35k (we can make up the gap from professional services discounts); $40k is a little outside standard, but I’ll escalate if procurement needs the full amount.

- Auto-convert legal: the conditional auto-convert (pilot -> committed term) requires a short addendum but is standard; legal will approve template language (no unusual R/W clauses).

Next steps:
1) I’ll send a two-page pilot addendum + concession table (will include metrics and the overage cap).
2) If SignalSuite signs by 31 Jul we’ll kick off onboarding immediately and reserve extra burst pool capacity for initial ramp.
3) I’ll also open a HubSpot deal record and send a DocuSign pilot addendum link.

Links: https://app.hubspot.com/records/DEAL-5482 (deal), https://www.docusign.com/templates/SS-pilot-2025

Camila Reyes
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments referenced: redwood_pricing_snapshot_v1.xlsx

title: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@medexahealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-07-14T09:18:00-07:00
Subject: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)

Hi Alicia — thanks again for your time this morning. Quick recap from the demo and the action items we discussed: 

- Summary: You walked us through your ingestion flow (EHR exports -> Medexa ETL -> anonymization step -> Redwood inference). Primary ask is minimizing retention of raw PII and providing fine-grained controls for audit logs.
- Questions you raised we promised to follow up on: 0-retention options for PII, what we log for debugging vs audit, and whether we can shorten logs for non-audit events.

Next steps we propose: 
1) I attached a short proposal (redwood-data-retention-proposal.pdf) showing two options: (A) transient-only (0 retention of input payloads after inference except for immediate KV cache lifetime), and (B) 7-day ephemeral retention with encrypted-at-rest fast-delete.
2) If option A still leaves you unable to meet compliance, we'll surface a formal exception process (medexa-retention-exception-form.docx) and loop in Rishi from Security to provide a written sign-off path.
3) Marcus will draft commercial guardrails for a pilot (SLA, price, and rollback).

Can you confirm which option you want to evaluate in a 30-day pilot? If you prefer, we can prepare a DocuSign-ready exception request for your security team.

Thanks — Marissa

--
Marissa Cole
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0112


From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@medexahealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Medexa Procurement <procurement@medexahealth.com>
Date: 2025-07-14T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)

Hi Marissa — appreciate the follow-up and the concise options. A couple clarifying questions before we pick Option A or B: 

1) 0-retention for raw PII: when you say "transient-only", does that include all debug logs and request metadata? Our compliance team insists that any logs that could be reconstructed with external tooling must be excluded from long-term storage.
2) Audit & eDiscovery: we need to retain evidence for certain events for 90 days. Can you separate audit logs (retained 90 days) from operational/debug logs (shortened retention)?
3) Technical proof: can your team provide a short-runbook of deletion guarantees (e.g., delete job cadence, truncated KV cache behavior, and any background replication windows)? We may need this for our internal audit.

If you can confirm separation of audit vs non-audit logs and provide that runbook, Procurement is willing to move to a pilot. We will likely request the exception form if Option A is chosen (0 retention for payloads).

Thanks — Alicia
Alicia Gomez
Director, Cloud Integrations | Medexa Health
alicia.gomez@medexahealth.com

To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-07-14T11:27:00-07:00

- confirmation that "transient-only" excludes any logs that could be reconstructed into PII after the fact
- 90-day retention for audit/evidence logs, separate from operational logs
- a short runbook describing deletion cadence, replication windows, and KV cache truncation behavior

I attached the medexa-retention-exception-form.docx that they mentioned. Happy to set a brief internal sync if needed.

Thanks — Marissa

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@medexahealth.com>
Date: 2025-07-14 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Medexa Procurement <procurement@medexahealth.com>

[original message quoted above]


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-07-15T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)

Marissa / Marcus — quick technical and compliance note to support the reply to Medexa. High level answers below; I also attached a one-page runbook to this thread (internal doc) — happy to convert to customer-friendly language if you want to send it out.

- 0-retention (Option A): We can offer a transient-only mode where payloads are not persisted beyond the inference operation. Constraints: we still produce ephemeral debug traces that exist only in-memory and are flushed on process termination; any background replication windows (for HA) are <=30 minutes and covered by encryption-in-transit policies. This mode requires customers to forgo server-side logging for full request payloads.
- Audit vs operational logs: We can implement a dual-retention model. Audit logs (authentication events, model version used, high-level request IDs) can be retained for 90 days. Operational/debug logs (stack traces, full request payloads) can be set to a configurable TTL — options: 0 days (never persisted), 7 days, or 30 days.
- Deletion runbook (summary): delete jobs run on a cron every hour for operational logs > TTL; a background purger processes KV cache truncation and zeroes memory-mapped pages before freeing; for encrypted snapshots we rotate keys and mark blobs for fast-delete. I'll add exact commands and retention cron samples if Medexa needs them for their audit packet.

Proposal for pilot: 
- Pilot period: 30 days
- Retention configuration: audit logs = 90 days; operational logs = 7 days (customer can request 0 days for specific routes via exception form)
- Exceptions: We can accept a signed exception request for route-level 0-retention on payloads, with an internal security review and an SLA amendment (limited support entitlement during exception window).

Marcus: if you can draft the pilot commercial note (30 days, reduced support SLA, and rollback plan), I'll prepare a customer-ready runbook and exception template.

Rishi
Head of Security & Compliance (acting) | Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai


From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@medexahealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Medexa Procurement <procurement@medexahealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-07-16T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)

Thanks everyone — the runbook language and dual-retention approach look workable. Procurement is authorized to proceed with a pilot under the following agreed constraints: 

- Pilot (30 days) under Deal MEDX-2025-114
- Retention: audit logs retained 90 days; operational logs set to 7 days by default
- Route exceptions: Medexa will submit exception forms for three high-risk routes requesting 0-retention for payloads during the pilot; exceptions require signed acknowledgement and reduced support SLA (as Rishi outlined)
- Deliverables: Redwood to provide the deletion runbook and the exception template in DocuSign format; procurement will sign on receipt.

Please send the DocuSign link and the final pilot terms; Procurement expects to have the signed pilot in place by next Wednesday.

Best — Alicia
Alicia Gomez
Director, Cloud Integrations | Medexa Health

To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Medexa Procurement <procurement@medexahealth.com>

[original message quoted above]


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-07-15T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)

Marissa / Marcus — quick technical and compliance note to support the reply to Medexa. High level answers below; I also attached a one-page runbook to this thread (internal doc) — happy to convert to customer-friendly language if you want to send it out.

- 0-retention (Option A): We can offer a transient-only mode where payloads are not persisted beyond the inference operation. Constraints: we still produce ephemeral debug traces that exist only in-memory and are flushed on process termination; any background replication windows (for HA) are <=30 minutes and covered by encryption-in-transit policies. This mode requires customers to forgo server-side logging for full request payloads.
- Audit vs operational logs: We can implement a dual-retention model. Audit logs (authentication events, model version used, high-level request IDs) can be retained for 90 days. Operational/debug logs (stack traces, full request payloads) can be set to a configurable TTL — options: 0 days (never persisted), 7 days, or 30 days.
- Deletion runbook (summary): delete jobs run on a cron every hour for operational logs > TTL; a background purger processes KV cache truncation and zeroes memory-mapped pages before freeing; for encrypted snapshots we rotate keys and mark blobs for fast-delete. I'll add exact commands and retention cron samples if Medexa needs them for their audit packet.

Proposal for pilot: 
- Pilot period: 30 days
- Retention configuration: audit logs = 90 days; operational logs = 7 days (customer can request 0 days for specific routes via exception form)
- Exceptions: We can accept a signed exception request for route-level 0-retention on payloads, with an internal security review and an SLA amendment (limited support entitlement during exception window).

Marcus: if you can draft the pilot commercial note (30 days, reduced support SLA, and rollback plan), I'll prepare a customer-ready runbook and exception template.

Rishi
Head of Security & Compliance (acting) | Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai


From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@medexahealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Medexa Procurement <procurement@medexahealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-07-16T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)

Thanks everyone — the runbook language and dual-retention approach look workable. Procurement is authorized to proceed with a pilot under the following agreed constraints: 

- Pilot (30 days) under Deal MEDX-2025-114
- Retention: audit logs retained 90 days; operational logs set to 7 days by default
- Route exceptions: Medexa will submit exception forms for three high-risk routes requesting 0-retention for payloads during the pilot; exceptions require signed acknowledgement and reduced support SLA (as Rishi outlined)
- Deliverables: Redwood to provide the deletion runbook and the exception template in DocuSign format; procurement will sign on receipt.

Please send the DocuSign link and the final pilot terms; Procurement expects to have the signed pilot in place by next Wednesday.

Best — Alicia
Alicia Gomez
Director, Cloud Integrations | Medexa Health
alicia.gomez@medexahealth.com

title: [LumenWorks] Request: queueing behavior review + temporary credits
preview:
From: Samira Patel <samira.patel@lumenworks.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@lumenworks.com
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Production impact—queueing spike, request for short-term credit/behavior change

Hi Alex,

We saw a significant increase in tail latency and queuing on our reserved pool during yesterday's batch of customer requests (roughly 18:10–18:40 PDT). Several API calls retried which impacted UX for a portion of our customers.

We understand bursts happen, but our contract references a dedicated throughput target and queueing behavior that should minimize this. Could Redwood help with two things?

1) A short-term credit for the token overage incurred during the incident window (rough estimate attached).
2) A quick rundown of what caused the queuing behavior and whether a temporary priority carveout or headroom change is possible while we stabilize our request shaping.

Attached: prod_request_logs_20250712.csv (request timestamps, latencies, pool id).

We'd like to keep things moving—can we get an initial response today?

Thanks,
Samira Patel
Ops Lead, LumenWorks
procurement@lumenworks.com

--
Attachment: prod_request_logs_20250712.csv (CSV, 84KB)

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Samira Patel <samira.patel@lumenworks.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production impact—queueing spike, request for short-term credit/behavior change

Hi Samira—thanks for the note and for sending the logs. I'm sorry you hit that window; I know how disruptive retries are for end users.

Quick summary from our side after an initial look at the CSV: there was a concentrated burst from a handful of downstream clients that caused concurrent inflight tokens to exceed your soft headroom threshold on pool RW-Dedicated-14. That triggered the queueing guard which preserves latency SLOs for the pool but pushes lower-priority inflight into the backlog.

Immediate options we can offer:
- Temporary credit for the incremental tokens during the incident window (we can propose a credit amount once Ben confirms billing calculation).
- A time-limited priority carveout for your application for the next 72 hours (limits apply; requires manager approval).
- Config guidance: short-term request shaping (we can share a snippet to reduce concurrency) and a parameterized autoscale bump if you'd like to accept higher cost for reduced queueing.

I've looped in Ben (billing) and Lauren (server capacity lead) to weigh in on credits and the carveout path. Can you confirm the exact pool id and the ingress keys used during the spike (if you have them), and whether you want the carveout window to be immediate?

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com

--
Attachment referenced: redwood_draft_sla_addendum_v1.docx (Draft language for temporary carveouts)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Samira Patel <samira.patel@lumenworks.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 15:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production impact—queueing spike, request for short-term credit/behavior change

Alex / Lauren—I've run the numbers against LumenWorks' usage. If we base the credit on the incremental token count during the incident window, I'm comfortable offering a one-time credit equal to 80% of the incremental token spend for the affected window (this is discretionary but keeps precedent minimal).

Notes:
- Credit calculation uses the attached CSV and excludes any obvious client-side retry loops (we flagged a few anomalous patterns).
- For the temporary priority carveout: operationally we can enable a 48-hour soft-priority lane scoped to application key lw-main-01 with immediate effect. It will allow a higher inflight headroom allocation but does consume pooled capacity and could slightly increase tail exposure for other tenants—hence why we recommend it only short-term.

Lauren: can you confirm we have capacity headroom in RW-Dedicated-14 to grant lw-main-01 a 48-hour carveout starting now? If yes I approve.

Samira—if you approve the credit and the 48-hour carveout, we'll prepare an SLA addendum and a DocuSign for signature. I'll include the credit amount in the Doc (estimated $1,900 based on our calc).

-Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

--
Attachment: redwood_draft_sla_addendum_v1.docx (Draft addendum with carveout window, credit clause)

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Samira Patel <samira.patel@lumenworks.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Jul 2025 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production impact—queueing spike, request for short-term credit/behavior change

Confirming capacity: RW-Dedicated-14 has sufficient transient headroom to support a 48-hour scoped priority lane for lw-main-01 starting immediately. Operations will enable the carveout at 11:30 PDT today; monitoring hooks will be in place and we'll rollback if cross-tenant impact is observed.

Ben: please proceed with the one-time credit DocuSign for $1,900 and include the carveout language in the SLA addendum. Samira—we'll send the DocuSign link shortly and will turn on the carveout when you confirm acceptance.

Alex: please move the thread to procurement & legal for quick signature; include the HubSpot deal link and the DocuSign.

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Director, Capacity Operations, Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.com

--
Action items: enable 48-hour carveout at 11:30 PDT; generate DocuSign with $1,900 credit; attach redwood_draft_sla_addendum_v1.docx.
